verses.kjv: And delivered him out of all his afflictions, and gave him favour and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh king of Egypt; and he made him governor over Egypt and all his house.

verses.greek: ἦλθεν δὲ λιμὸς ἐφ’ ὅλην τὴν ⸀Αἴγυπτον καὶ Χανάαν καὶ θλῖψις μεγάλη, καὶ οὐχ ηὕρισκον χορτάσματα οἱ πατέρες ἡμῶν·
verses.mistral: Famine spread over all Aigypton and Chanaan, and great was the distress, so that our fathers found no food.
verses.kjv: Now there came a dearth over all the land of Egypt and Chanaan, and great affliction: and our fathers found no sustenance.

verses.greek: ἀκούσας δὲ Ἰακὼβ ὄντα ⸂σιτία εἰς Αἴγυπτον⸃ ἐξαπέστειλεν τοὺς πατέρας ἡμῶν πρῶτον·
verses.mistral: Sends first our fathers to Egypt for food.
verses.kjv: But when Jacob heard that there was corn in Egypt, he sent out our fathers first.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐν τῷ δευτέρῳ ⸀ἀνεγνωρίσθη Ἰωσὴφ τοῖς ἀδελφοῖς αὐτοῦ, καὶ φανερὸν ἐγένετο τῷ Φαραὼ τὸ ⸀γένος Ἰωσήφ.
verses.mistral: Joseph was revealed to his brothers, and it became apparent to Pharaoh that Joseph's lineage was from him.
verses.kjv: And at the second time Joseph was made known to his brethren; and Joseph's kindred was made known unto Pharaoh.

verses.greek: ἀποστείλας δὲ Ἰωσὴφ μετεκαλέσατο ⸂Ἰακὼβ τὸν πατέρα αὐτοῦ⸃ καὶ πᾶσαν τὴν συγγένειαν ἐν ψυχαῖς ἑβδομήκοντα πέντε,
verses.mistral: Sends Joseph called Jacob his father and all his kindred seventy-five
verses.kjv: Then sent Joseph, and called his father Jacob to him, and all his kindred, threescore and fifteen souls.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ κατέβη⸃ Ἰακὼβ εἰς Αἴγυπτον. καὶ ἐτελεύτησεν αὐτὸς καὶ οἱ πατέρες ἡμῶν,
verses.mistral: And Jacob went into Egypt. And he died there, along with our ancestors.
verses.kjv: So Jacob went down into Egypt, and died, he, and our fathers,

verses.greek: καὶ μετετέθησαν εἰς Συχὲμ καὶ ἐτέθησαν ἐν τῷ μνήματι ⸀ᾧ ὠνήσατο Ἀβραὰμ τιμῆς ἀργυρίου παρὰ τῶν υἱῶν Ἑμμὼρ ⸀ἐν Συχέμ.
verses.mistral: And they were laid in the tomb that Abraham had purchased from the sons of Hemor in Shechem.
verses.kjv: And were carried over into Sychem, and laid in the sepulchre that Abraham bought for a sum of money of the sons of Emmor the father of Sychem.

verses.greek: Καθὼς δὲ ἤγγιζεν ὁ χρόνος τῆς ἐπαγγελίας ἧς ⸀ὡμολόγησεν ὁ θεὸς τῷ Ἀβραάμ, ηὔξησεν ὁ λαὸς καὶ ἐπληθύνθη ἐν Αἰγύπτῳ,
verses.mistral: As the time of the fulfillment of the promise that God made to Abraham drew near, the people multiplied and increased in Egypt
verses.kjv: But when the time of the promise drew nigh, which God had sworn to Abraham, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt,

verses.greek: ἄχρι οὗ ἀνέστη βασιλεὺς ἕτερος ⸂ἐπ’ Αἴγυπτον⸃, ὃς οὐκ ᾔδει τὸν Ἰωσήφ.
verses.mistral: A new king arose over Egypt, who did not know Joseph.
verses.kjv: Till another king arose, which knew not Joseph.

verses.greek: οὗτος κατασοφισάμενος τὸ γένος ἡμῶν ἐκάκωσεν τοὺς ⸀πατέρας τοῦ ποιεῖν ⸂τὰ βρέφη ἔκθετα⸃ αὐτῶν εἰς τὸ μὴ ζῳογονεῖσθαι.
verses.mistral: This ruler ordered our ancestors to throw their newborn children away, so they would not survive.
verses.kjv: The same dealt subtilly with our kindred, and evil entreated our fathers, so that they cast out their young children, to the end they might not live.

verses.greek: ἐν ᾧ καιρῷ ἐγεννήθη Μωϋσῆς, καὶ ἦν ἀστεῖος τῷ θεῷ· ὃς ἀνετράφη μῆνας τρεῖς ἐν τῷ οἴκῳ τοῦ πατρός·
verses.mistral: Moses was born in that time, and he was esteemed by God. He was nursed for three months in his father's house.
verses.kjv: In which time Moses was born, and was exceeding fair, and nourished up in his father's house three months:

verses.greek: ⸂ἐκτεθέντος δὲ αὐτοῦ ἀνείλατο αὐτὸν⸃ ἡ θυγάτηρ Φαραὼ καὶ ἀνεθρέψατο αὐτὸν ἑαυτῇ εἰς υἱόν.
verses.mistral: The daughter of Pharaoh took him and raised him as her own son.
verses.kjv: And when he was cast out, Pharaoh's daughter took him up, and nourished him for her own son.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐπαιδεύθη ⸀Μωϋσῆς πάσῃ σοφίᾳ Αἰγυπτίων, ἦν δὲ δυνατὸς ἐν λόγοις καὶ ἔργοις ⸀αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Moses was educated in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and he was powerful in words and deeds.
verses.kjv: And Moses was learned in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and was mighty in words and in deeds.

verses.greek: Ὡς δὲ ἐπληροῦτο αὐτῷ τεσσερακονταετὴς χρόνος, ἀνέβη ἐπὶ τὴν καρδίαν αὐτοῦ ἐπισκέψασθαι τοὺς ἀδελφοὺς αὐτοῦ τοὺς υἱοὺς Ἰσραήλ.
verses.mistral: As he turned forty years old, the thought to visit his brethren, the children of Israel, arose in his heart.
verses.kjv: And when he was full forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his brethren the children of Israel.

verses.greek: καὶ ἰδών τινα ἀδικούμενον ἠμύνατο καὶ ἐποίησεν ἐκδίκησιν τῷ καταπονουμένῳ πατάξας τὸν Αἰγύπτιον.
verses.mistral: Defended a wronged man, making justice, and struck the Egyptian.
verses.kjv: And seeing one of them suffer wrong, he defended him, and avenged him that was oppressed, and smote the Egyptian:

verses.greek: ἐνόμιζεν δὲ συνιέναι τοὺς ⸀ἀδελφοὺς ὅτι ὁ θεὸς διὰ χειρὸς αὐτοῦ δίδωσιν ⸂σωτηρίαν αὐτοῖς⸃, οἱ δὲ οὐ συνῆκαν.
verses.mistral: Believed however the brothers that God through his hands gives them salvation, but they did not agree.
verses.kjv: For he supposed his brethren would have understood how that God by his hand would deliver them: but they understood not.

verses.greek: τῇ τε ἐπιούσῃ ἡμέρᾳ ὤφθη αὐτοῖς μαχομένοις καὶ ⸀συνήλλασσεν αὐτοὺς εἰς εἰρήνην εἰπών· Ἄνδρες, ἀδελφοί ⸀ἐστε· ἱνατί ἀδικεῖτε ἀλλήλους;
verses.mistral: On the next day they encountered them and tried to convince them, saying, "Men, brothers, why are you causing distress and trouble among each other?"
verses.kjv: And the next day he shewed himself unto them as they strove, and would have set them at one again, saying, Sirs, ye are brethren; why do ye wrong one to another?

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀδικῶν τὸν πλησίον ἀπώσατο αὐτὸν εἰπών· Τίς σε κατέστησεν ἄρχοντα καὶ δικαστὴν ἐφ’ ⸀ἡμῶν;
verses.mistral: Who made you a ruler and judge over us?
verses.kjv: But he that did his neighbour wrong thrust him away, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge over us?

verses.greek: μὴ ἀνελεῖν με σὺ θέλεις ὃν τρόπον ἀνεῖλες ⸀ἐχθὲς τὸν Αἰγύπτιον;
verses.mistral: Do you want to kill me, as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?
verses.kjv: Wilt thou kill me, as thou diddest the Egyptian yesterday?

verses.greek: ἔφυγεν δὲ Μωϋσῆς ἐν τῷ λόγῳ τούτῳ, καὶ ἐγένετο πάροικος ἐν γῇ Μαδιάμ, οὗ ἐγέννησεν υἱοὺς δύο.
verses.mistral: Moses fled at this word, and became a dweller in the land of Midian, where he fathered two sons.
verses.kjv: Then fled Moses at this saying, and was a stranger in the land of Madian, where he begat two sons.

verses.greek: Καὶ πληρωθέντων ἐτῶν τεσσεράκοντα ὤφθη αὐτῷ ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ τοῦ ὄρους Σινᾶ ⸀ἄγγελος ἐν φλογὶ πυρὸς βάτου·
verses.mistral: Appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount Sinai an angel of the Lord in a flame of fire within a bush
verses.kjv: And when forty years were expired, there appeared to him in the wilderness of mount Sina an angel of the Lord in a flame of fire in a bush.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Μωϋσῆς ἰδὼν ⸀ἐθαύμασεν τὸ ὅραμα. προσερχομένου δὲ αὐτοῦ κατανοῆσαι ἐγένετο φωνὴ ⸀κυρίου·
verses.mistral: Moses, seeing the sight, marveled. Upon approaching to look more closely, there was a voice of the Lord.
verses.kjv: When Moses saw it, he wondered at the sight: and as he drew near to behold it, the voice of the Lord came unto him,

verses.greek: Ἐγὼ ὁ θεὸς τῶν πατέρων σου, ὁ θεὸς Ἀβραὰμ ⸀καὶ Ἰσαὰκ ⸁καὶ Ἰακώβ. ἔντρομος δὲ γενόμενος Μωϋσῆς οὐκ ἐτόλμα κατανοῆσαι.
verses.mistral: The Lord, the God of your fathers, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, appeared to Moses. And being exceedingly afraid, Moses was not able to look upon it.
verses.kjv: Saying, I am the God of thy fathers, the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. Then Moses trembled, and durst not behold.

verses.greek: εἶπεν δὲ αὐτῷ ὁ κύριος· Λῦσον τὸ ὑπόδημα τῶν ποδῶν σου, ὁ γὰρ τόπος ⸀ἐφ’ ᾧ ἕστηκας γῆ ἁγία ἐστίν.
verses.mistral: The Lord said to him, "Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place on which you stand is holy ground."
verses.kjv: Then said the Lord to him, Put off thy shoes from thy feet: for the place where thou standest is holy ground.

verses.greek: ἰδὼν εἶδον τὴν κάκωσιν τοῦ λαοῦ μου τοῦ ἐν Αἰγύπτῳ, καὶ τοῦ στεναγμοῦ ⸀αὐτοῦ ἤκουσα, καὶ κατέβην ἐξελέσθαι αὐτούς· καὶ νῦν δεῦρο ⸀ἀποστείλω σε εἰς Αἴγυπτον.
verses.mistral: I have seen the affliction of my people in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning, and I have come down to deliver them. Now go, for I am sending you to Egypt.
verses.kjv: I have seen, I have seen the affliction of my people which is in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning, and am come down to deliver them. And now come, I will send thee into Egypt.

verses.greek: Τοῦτον τὸν Μωϋσῆν, ὃν ἠρνήσαντο εἰπόντες· Τίς σε κατέστησεν ἄρχοντα καὶ δικαστήν, τοῦτον ὁ θεὸς ⸀καὶ ἄρχοντα καὶ λυτρωτὴν ⸂ἀπέσταλκεν σὺν⸃ χειρὶ ἀγγέλου τοῦ ὀφθέντος αὐτῷ ἐν τῇ βάτῳ.
verses.mistral: This Moses, whom they disowned saying, "Who has made you a ruler and a judge?" this God sent, with the hand of an angel appearing to him in the burning bush.
verses.kjv: This Moses whom they refused, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge? the same did God send to be a ruler and a deliverer by the hand of the angel which appeared to him in the bush.

verses.greek: οὗτος ἐξήγαγεν αὐτοὺς ποιήσας τέρατα καὶ σημεῖα ἐν ⸀γῇ Αἰγύπτῳ καὶ ἐν Ἐρυθρᾷ Θαλάσσῃ καὶ ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ ἔτη τεσσεράκοντα.
verses.mistral: This one led them out, performing wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, the Red Sea, and the wilderness for forty years.
verses.kjv: He brought them out, after that he had shewed wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, and in the Red sea, and in the wilderness forty years.

verses.greek: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ Μωϋσῆς ὁ ⸀εἴπας τοῖς υἱοῖς Ἰσραήλ· Προφήτην ὑμῖν ἀναστήσει ⸂ὁ θεὸς⸃ ἐκ τῶν ἀδελφῶν ὑμῶν ὡς ἐμέ.
verses.mistral: This is Moses who said to the children of Israel, "A prophet God will raise up for you from among your brothers like me."
verses.kjv: ¶ This is that Moses, which said unto the children of Israel, A prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear.

verses.greek: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ γενόμενος ἐν τῇ ἐκκλησίᾳ ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ μετὰ τοῦ ἀγγέλου τοῦ λαλοῦντος αὐτῷ ἐν τῷ ὄρει Σινᾶ καὶ τῶν πατέρων ἡμῶν, ὃς ἐδέξατο ⸀λόγια ζῶντα δοῦναι ⸀ἡμῖν,
verses.mistral: This is the One who was in the wilderness with the angel speaking to him in Mount Sinai, and to our fathers, who received living words to give to us.
verses.kjv: This is he, that was in the church in the wilderness with the angel which spake to him in the mount Sina, and with our fathers: who received the lively oracles to give unto us:

verses.greek: ᾧ οὐκ ἠθέλησαν ὑπήκοοι γενέσθαι οἱ πατέρες ἡμῶν, ἀλλὰ ἀπώσαντο καὶ ἐστράφησαν ⸂ἐν ταῖς καρδίαις⸃ αὐτῶν εἰς Αἴγυπτον,
verses.mistral: Refused to be obedient were our fathers, but they turned away and turned into Egypt.
verses.kjv: To whom our fathers would not obey, but thrust him from them, and in their hearts turned back again into Egypt,

verses.greek: εἰπόντες τῷ Ἀαρών· Ποίησον ἡμῖν θεοὺς οἳ προπορεύσονται ἡμῶν· ὁ γὰρ Μωϋσῆς οὗτος, ὃς ἐξήγαγεν ἡμᾶς ἐκ γῆς Αἰγύπτου, οὐκ οἴδαμεν τί ⸀ἐγένετο αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: Said to Aaron: "Make us gods who shall go before us, for as for this Moses who brought us out from the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him."
verses.kjv: Saying unto Aaron, Make us gods to go before us: for as for this Moses, which brought us out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐμοσχοποίησαν ἐν ταῖς ἡμέραις ἐκείναις καὶ ἀνήγαγον θυσίαν τῷ εἰδώλῳ, καὶ εὐφραίνοντο ἐν τοῖς ἔργοις τῶν χειρῶν αὐτῶν.
verses.mistral: And they made offerings to the idol in those days, and they rejoiced in the work of their hands.
verses.kjv: And they made a calf in those days, and offered sacrifice unto the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their own hands.

verses.greek: ἔστρεψεν δὲ ὁ θεὸς καὶ παρέδωκεν αὐτοὺς λατρεύειν τῇ στρατιᾷ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ, καθὼς γέγραπται ἐν βίβλῳ τῶν προφητῶν· Μὴ σφάγια καὶ θυσίας προσηνέγκατέ μοι ἔτη τεσσεράκοντα ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ, οἶκος Ἰσραήλ;
verses.mistral: Turned God, and He gave them to serve the army of heaven, as it is written in the book of the prophets: "Have I not given you slaughtered offerings and sacrifices for forty years in the wilderness, O house of Israel?"
verses.kjv: Then God turned, and gave them up to worship the host of heaven; as it is written in the book of the prophets, O ye house of Israel, have ye offered to me slain beasts and sacrifices by the space of forty years in the wilderness?

verses.greek: καὶ ἀνελάβετε τὴν σκηνὴν τοῦ Μολὸχ καὶ τὸ ἄστρον τοῦ ⸀θεοῦ ⸀Ῥαιφάν, τοὺς τύπους οὓς ἐποιήσατε προσκυνεῖν αὐτοῖς. καὶ μετοικιῶ ὑμᾶς ἐπέκεινα Βαβυλῶνος.
verses.mistral: And you took the tent of Moloch and the star of their God Raimah, the images that you made to worship them. And I will displace you beyond Babylon.
verses.kjv: Yea, ye took up the tabernacle of Moloch, and the star of your god Remphan, figures which ye made to worship them: and I will carry you away beyond Babylon.

verses.greek: Ἡ σκηνὴ τοῦ μαρτυρίου ἦν τοῖς πατράσιν ἡμῶν ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ, καθὼς διετάξατο ὁ λαλῶν τῷ Μωϋσῇ ποιῆσαι αὐτὴν κατὰ τὸν τύπον ὃν ἑωράκει,
verses.mistral: The tent of witness was among our forefathers in the wilderness, as it was commanded by the one speaking to Moses to make it according to the pattern he had seen.
verses.kjv: Our fathers had the tabernacle of witness in the wilderness, as he had appointed, speaking unto Moses, that he should make it according to the fashion that he had seen.

verses.greek: ἣν καὶ εἰσήγαγον διαδεξάμενοι οἱ πατέρες ἡμῶν μετὰ Ἰησοῦ ἐν τῇ κατασχέσει τῶν ἐθνῶν ὧν ἐξῶσεν ὁ θεὸς ἀπὸ προσώπου τῶν πατέρων ἡμῶν ἕως τῶν ἡμερῶν Δαυίδ·
verses.mistral: Brought it in following our forefathers with Jesus in the possession of the nations, which God led out from before the faces of our fathers until the days of David.
verses.kjv: Which also our fathers that came after brought in with Jesus into the possession of the Gentiles, whom God drave out before the face of our fathers, unto the days of David;

verses.greek: ὃς εὗρεν χάριν ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ ᾐτήσατο εὑρεῖν σκήνωμα τῷ ⸀θεῷ Ἰακώβ.
verses.mistral: Found grace in the presence of God and asked to find a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
verses.kjv: Who found favour before God, and desired to find a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.

verses.greek: Σολομῶν δὲ οἰκοδόμησεν αὐτῷ οἶκον.
verses.mistral: Solomon built him a house.
verses.kjv: But Solomon built him an house.

verses.greek: ἀλλ’ οὐχ ὁ ὕψιστος ἐν ⸀χειροποιήτοις κατοικεῖ· καθὼς ὁ προφήτης λέγει·
verses.mistral: Highest does not dwell in temples As the prophet says
verses.kjv: Howbeit the most High dwelleth not in temples made with hands; as saith the prophet,

verses.greek: Ὁ οὐρανός μοι θρόνος, ⸂ἡ δὲ⸃ γῆ ὑποπόδιον τῶν ποδῶν μου· ποῖον οἶκον οἰκοδομήσετέ μοι, λέγει κύριος, ἢ τίς τόπος τῆς καταπαύσεώς μου;
verses.mistral: Heaven is my throne, and earth is the footstool for my feet. Where shall I rest, says the Lord, or who is the place of my repose?
verses.kjv: Heaven is my throne, and earth is my footstool: what house will ye build me? saith the Lord: or what is the place of my rest?

verses.greek: οὐχὶ ἡ χείρ μου ἐποίησεν ταῦτα πάντα;
verses.mistral: Did not my hand make these things?
verses.kjv: Hath not my hand made all these things?

verses.greek: Σκληροτράχηλοι καὶ ἀπερίτμητοι ⸀καρδίαις καὶ τοῖς ὠσίν, ὑμεῖς ἀεὶ τῷ πνεύματι τῷ ἁγίῳ ἀντιπίπτετε, ὡς οἱ πατέρες ὑμῶν καὶ ὑμεῖς.
verses.mistral: Stubborn and uncircumcised in heart and ears, you always resist the Holy Spirit; just as your fathers did, so do you.
verses.kjv: ¶ Ye stiffnecked and uncircumcised in heart and ears, ye do always resist the Holy Ghost: as your fathers did, so do ye.

verses.greek: τίνα τῶν προφητῶν οὐκ ἐδίωξαν οἱ πατέρες ὑμῶν; καὶ ἀπέκτειναν τοὺς προκαταγγείλαντας περὶ τῆς ἐλεύσεως τοῦ δικαίου οὗ νῦν ὑμεῖς προδόται καὶ φονεῖς ⸀ἐγένεσθε,
verses.mistral: Your fathers did not kill which of the prophets; and you have become their murderers and betrayers,
verses.kjv: Which of the prophets have not your fathers persecuted? and they have slain them which shewed before of the coming of the Just One; of whom ye have been now the betrayers and murderers:

verses.greek: οἵτινες ἐλάβετε τὸν νόμον εἰς διαταγὰς ἀγγέλων, καὶ οὐκ ἐφυλάξατε.
verses.mistral: You received the law given by angels, and did not keep it.
verses.kjv: Who have received the law by the disposition of angels, and have not kept it.

verses.greek: Ἀκούοντες δὲ ταῦτα διεπρίοντο ταῖς καρδίαις αὐτῶν καὶ ἔβρυχον τοὺς ὀδόντας ἐπ’ αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: Hearing this, they tore their clothes and shouted at him.
verses.kjv: ¶ When they heard these things, they were cut to the heart, and they gnashed on him with their teeth.

verses.greek: ὑπάρχων δὲ πλήρης πνεύματος ἁγίου ἀτενίσας εἰς τὸν οὐρανὸν εἶδεν δόξαν θεοῦ καὶ Ἰησοῦν ἑστῶτα ἐκ δεξιῶν τοῦ θεοῦ,
verses.mistral: Present, filled with the Holy Spirit, gazing into heaven, he saw God's glory and Jesus standing at the right hand of God.
verses.kjv: But he, being full of the Holy Ghost, looked up stedfastly into heaven, and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing on the right hand of God,

verses.greek: καὶ εἶπεν· Ἰδοὺ θεωρῶ τοὺς οὐρανοὺς ⸀διηνοιγμένους καὶ τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐκ δεξιῶν ἑστῶτα τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: I see heaven opened and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God.
verses.kjv: And said, Behold, I see the heavens opened, and the Son of man standing on the right hand of God.

verses.greek: κράξαντες δὲ φωνῇ μεγάλῃ συνέσχον τὰ ὦτα αὐτῶν, καὶ ὥρμησαν ὁμοθυμαδὸν ἐπ’ αὐτόν,
verses.mistral: Shouting with a great voice, they stopped their ears, and ran together against him.
verses.kjv: Then they cried out with a loud voice, and stopped their ears, and ran upon him with one accord,

verses.greek: καὶ ἐκβαλόντες ἔξω τῆς πόλεως ἐλιθοβόλουν. καὶ οἱ μάρτυρες ἀπέθεντο τὰ ἱμάτια ⸀αὐτῶν παρὰ τοὺς πόδας νεανίου καλουμένου Σαύλου.
verses.mistral: Throwing him out of the city, they pelted with stones. And the disciples cast their garments at the feet of the young man named Saul.
verses.kjv: And cast him out of the city, and stoned him: and the witnesses laid down their clothes at a young man's feet, whose name was Saul.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐλιθοβόλουν τὸν Στέφανον ἐπικαλούμενον καὶ λέγοντα· Κύριε Ἰησοῦ, δέξαι τὸ πνεῦμά μου·
verses.mistral: Stone the Stephen, calling out and saying: Lord Jesus, receive my spirit
verses.kjv: And they stoned Stephen, calling upon God, and saying, Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.

verses.greek: θεὶς δὲ τὰ γόνατα ἔκραξεν φωνῇ μεγάλῃ· Κύριε, μὴ στήσῃς αὐτοῖς ⸂ταύτην τὴν ἁμαρτίαν⸃· καὶ τοῦτο εἰπὼν ἐκοιμήθη.
verses.mistral: Cried out with a loud voice, "Lord, do not hold this sin against them." And having said this, he breathed his last.
verses.kjv: And he kneeled down, and cried with a loud voice, Lord, lay not this sin to their charge. And when he had said this, he fell asleep.

verses.greek: Σαῦλος δὲ ἦν συνευδοκῶν τῇ ἀναιρέσει αὐτοῦ. Ἐγένετο δὲ ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ διωγμὸς μέγας ἐπὶ τὴν ἐκκλησίαν τὴν ἐν Ἱεροσολύμοις· πάντες δὲ διεσπάρησαν κατὰ τὰς χώρας τῆς Ἰουδαίας καὶ Σαμαρείας πλὴν τῶν ἀποστόλων.
verses.mistral: Saul was consenting to his own death. On that day a great persecution broke out against the church in Jerusalem, and all except the apostles scattered throughout the land of Judea and Samaria.
verses.kjv: And Saul was consenting unto his death. And at that time there was a great persecution against the church which was at Jerusalem; and they were all scattered abroad throughout the regions of Judaea and Samaria, except the apostles.

verses.greek: συνεκόμισαν δὲ τὸν Στέφανον ἄνδρες εὐλαβεῖς καὶ ⸀ἐποίησαν κοπετὸν μέγαν ἐπ’ αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: Men of faith carried away Stephen, and they made a great mistreatment of him.
verses.kjv: And devout men carried Stephen to his burial, and made great lamentation over him.

verses.greek: Σαῦλος δὲ ἐλυμαίνετο τὴν ἐκκλησίαν κατὰ τοὺς οἴκους εἰσπορευόμενος, σύρων τε ἄνδρας καὶ γυναῖκας παρεδίδου εἰς φυλακήν.
verses.mistral: Samuel was disturbing the church by entering houses, dragging both men and women and putting them in prison.
verses.kjv: As for Saul, he made havock of the church, entering into every house, and haling men and women committed them to prison.

verses.greek: Οἱ μὲν οὖν διασπαρέντες διῆλθον εὐαγγελιζόμενοι τὸν λόγον.
verses.mistral: The men, spreading out, went on proclaiming the word.
verses.kjv: Therefore they that were scattered abroad went every where preaching the word.

verses.greek: Φίλιππος δὲ κατελθὼν εἰς ⸀τὴν πόλιν τῆς Σαμαρείας ἐκήρυσσεν αὐτοῖς τὸν χριστόν.
verses.mistral: Philip went to the city of Samaria and proclaimed Christ to them.
verses.kjv: Then Philip went down to the city of Samaria, and preached Christ unto them.

verses.greek: προσεῖχον ⸀δὲ οἱ ὄχλοι τοῖς λεγομένοις ὑπὸ τοῦ Φιλίππου ὁμοθυμαδὸν ἐν τῷ ἀκούειν αὐτοὺς καὶ βλέπειν τὰ σημεῖα ἃ ἐποίει·
verses.mistral: The crowd paid heed to what was said by Philip, as one, in hearing and seeing the miracles he performed.
verses.kjv: And the people with one accord gave heed unto those things which Philip spake, hearing and seeing the miracles which he did.

verses.greek: ⸀πολλοὶ γὰρ τῶν ἐχόντων πνεύματα ἀκάθαρτα βοῶντα φωνῇ μεγάλῃ ⸀ἐξήρχοντο, πολλοὶ δὲ παραλελυμένοι καὶ χωλοὶ ἐθεραπεύθησαν·
verses.mistral: Many with unclean spirits shouted aloud, and many who were paralyzed or crippled were healed.
verses.kjv: For unclean spirits, crying with loud voice, came out of many that were possessed with them: and many taken with palsies, and that were lame, were healed.

verses.greek: ⸂ἐγένετο δὲ⸃ ⸂πολλὴ χαρὰ⸃ ἐν τῇ πόλει ἐκείνῃ.
verses.mistral: Great joy arose in that city.
verses.kjv: And there was great joy in that city.

verses.greek: Ἀνὴρ δέ τις ὀνόματι Σίμων προϋπῆρχεν ἐν τῇ πόλει μαγεύων καὶ ⸀ἐξιστάνων τὸ ἔθνος τῆς Σαμαρείας, λέγων εἶναί τινα ἑαυτὸν μέγαν,
verses.mistral: Man from Samaria named Simon appeared in the city, practicing magic and astonishing the Samaritans, claiming to be someone great
verses.kjv: But there was a certain man, called Simon, which beforetime in the same city used sorcery, and bewitched the people of Samaria, giving out that himself was some great one:

verses.greek: ᾧ προσεῖχον ⸀πάντες ἀπὸ μικροῦ ἕως μεγάλου λέγοντες· Οὗτός ἐστιν ἡ Δύναμις τοῦ θεοῦ ἡ ⸀καλουμένη Μεγάλη.
verses.mistral: This one is the Power of God, called Great.
verses.kjv: To whom they all gave heed, from the least to the greatest, saying, This man is the great power of God.

verses.greek: προσεῖχον δὲ αὐτῷ διὰ τὸ ἱκανῷ χρόνῳ ταῖς μαγείαις ἐξεστακέναι αὐτούς.
verses.mistral: Adhered to him, however, through sufficient time, their magic was found to fail.
verses.kjv: And to him they had regard, because that of long time he had bewitched them with sorceries.

verses.greek: ὅτε δὲ ἐπίστευσαν τῷ Φιλίππῳ εὐαγγελιζομένῳ ⸀περὶ τῆς βασιλείας τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ τοῦ ὀνόματος Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ, ἐβαπτίζοντο ἄνδρες τε καὶ γυναῖκες.
verses.mistral: When they believed Philip preaching concerning the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, men as well as women.
verses.kjv: But when they believed Philip preaching the things concerning the kingdom of God, and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Σίμων καὶ αὐτὸς ἐπίστευσεν, καὶ βαπτισθεὶς ἦν προσκαρτερῶν τῷ Φιλίππῳ, θεωρῶν τε ⸂σημεῖα καὶ δυνάμεις μεγάλας γινομένας⸃ ἐξίστατο.
verses.mistral: Simon himself believed, and after being baptized, he continued with Philip, observing signs and great wonders taking place.
verses.kjv: Then Simon himself believed also: and when he was baptized, he continued with Philip, and wondered, beholding the miracles and signs which were done.

verses.greek: Ἀκούσαντες δὲ οἱ ἐν Ἱεροσολύμοις ἀπόστολοι ὅτι δέδεκται ἡ Σαμάρεια τὸν λόγον τοῦ θεοῦ, ἀπέστειλαν πρὸς ⸀αὐτοὺς Πέτρον καὶ Ἰωάννην,
verses.mistral: They heard in Jerusalem that the Samaritans had received the word of God, and they sent Peter and John to them.
verses.kjv: Now when the apostles which were at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent unto them Peter and John:

verses.greek: οἵτινες καταβάντες προσηύξαντο περὶ αὐτῶν ὅπως λάβωσιν πνεῦμα ἅγιον·
verses.mistral: Upon their arrival, they prayed for them to receive the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: Who, when they were come down, prayed for them, that they might receive the Holy Ghost:

verses.greek: ⸀οὐδέπω γὰρ ἦν ἐπ’ οὐδενὶ αὐτῶν ἐπιπεπτωκός, μόνον δὲ βεβαπτισμένοι ὑπῆρχον εἰς τὸ ὄνομα τοῦ ⸀κυρίου Ἰησοῦ.
verses.mistral: Not yet was anyone of them baptized, but they were only named after the Lord Jesus.
verses.kjv: (For as yet he was fallen upon none of them: only they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.)

verses.greek: τότε ⸀ἐπετίθεσαν τὰς χεῖρας ἐπ’ αὐτούς, καὶ ἐλάμβανον πνεῦμα ἅγιον.
verses.mistral: Then they laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: Then laid they their hands on them, and they received the Holy Ghost.

verses.greek: ⸀ἰδὼν δὲ ὁ Σίμων ὅτι διὰ τῆς ἐπιθέσεως τῶν χειρῶν τῶν ἀποστόλων δίδοται τὸ ⸀πνεῦμα προσήνεγκεν αὐτοῖς χρήματα
verses.mistral: Receiving the money, Simon saw that through the laying on of the apostles' hands the Holy Spirit was given.
verses.kjv: And when Simon saw that through laying on of the apostles' hands the Holy Ghost was given, he offered them money,

verses.greek: λέγων· Δότε κἀμοὶ τὴν ἐξουσίαν ταύτην ἵνα ᾧ ἐὰν ἐπιθῶ τὰς χεῖρας λαμβάνῃ πνεῦμα ἅγιον.
verses.mistral: Grant me also this authority, that to whom I might put my hands he may receive the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: Saying, Give me also this power, that on whomsoever I lay hands, he may receive the Holy Ghost.

verses.greek: Πέτρος δὲ εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτόν· Τὸ ἀργύριόν σου σὺν σοὶ εἴη εἰς ἀπώλειαν ὅτι τὴν δωρεὰν τοῦ θεοῦ ἐνόμισας διὰ χρημάτων κτᾶσθαι.
verses.mistral: Peter said to him, "Your silver be with you for destruction because you thought that the gift of God could be bought with money."
verses.kjv: But Peter said unto him, Thy money perish with thee, because thou hast thought that the gift of God may be purchased with money.

verses.greek: οὐκ ἔστιν σοι μερὶς οὐδὲ κλῆρος ἐν τῷ λόγῳ τούτῳ, ἡ γὰρ καρδία σου οὐκ ἔστιν εὐθεῖα ⸀ἔναντι τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: Your portion or inheritance is not in this word, for your heart is not right before God.
verses.kjv: Thou hast neither part nor lot in this matter: for thy heart is not right in the sight of God.

verses.greek: μετανόησον οὖν ἀπὸ τῆς κακίας σου ταύτης, καὶ δεήθητι τοῦ ⸀κυρίου εἰ ἄρα ἀφεθήσεταί σοι ἡ ἐπίνοια τῆς καρδίας σου·
verses.mistral: Repent therefore from this wickedness of yours, and pray to the Lord if perhaps He will forgive you the intention of your heart.
verses.kjv: Repent therefore of this thy wickedness, and pray God, if perhaps the thought of thine heart may be forgiven thee.

verses.greek: εἰς γὰρ χολὴν πικρίας καὶ σύνδεσμον ἀδικίας ὁρῶ σε ὄντα.
verses.mistral: You are seeing me full of anger and bound by injustice.
verses.kjv: For I perceive that thou art in the gall of bitterness, and in the bond of iniquity.

verses.greek: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ Σίμων εἶπεν· Δεήθητε ὑμεῖς ὑπὲρ ἐμοῦ πρὸς τὸν κύριον ὅπως μηδὲν ἐπέλθῃ ἐπ’ ἐμὲ ὧν εἰρήκατε.
verses.mistral: Simon replied, "Ask the Lord for me that nothing of what you have said may come upon me."
verses.kjv: Then answered Simon, and said, Pray ye to the Lord for me, that none of these things which ye have spoken come upon me.

verses.greek: Οἱ μὲν οὖν διαμαρτυράμενοι καὶ λαλήσαντες τὸν λόγον τοῦ κυρίου ⸀ὑπέστρεφον εἰς ⸀Ἱεροσόλυμα, πολλάς τε κώμας τῶν Σαμαριτῶν ⸀εὐηγγελίζοντο.
verses.mistral: Returned to Jerusalem, they testified about the Lord's message and preached the Good News in many Samaritan villages.
verses.kjv: And they, when they had testified and preached the word of the Lord, returned to Jerusalem, and preached the gospel in many villages of the Samaritans.

verses.greek: Ἄγγελος δὲ κυρίου ἐλάλησεν πρὸς Φίλιππον λέγων· Ἀνάστηθι καὶ πορεύου κατὰ μεσημβρίαν ἐπὶ τὴν ὁδὸν τὴν καταβαίνουσαν ἀπὸ Ἰερουσαλὴμ εἰς Γάζαν· αὕτη ἐστὶν ἔρημος.
verses.mistral: Angel of the Lord spoke to Philip saying, "Arise and go toward the south along the road that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza. This is a wilderness."
verses.kjv: And the angel of the Lord spake unto Philip, saying, Arise, and go toward the south unto the way that goeth down from Jerusalem unto Gaza, which is desert.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀναστὰς ἐπορεύθη, καὶ ἰδοὺ ἀνὴρ Αἰθίοψ εὐνοῦχος δυνάστης ⸀Κανδάκης βασιλίσσης Αἰθιόπων, ὃς ἦν ἐπὶ πάσης τῆς γάζης αὐτῆς, ὃς ἐληλύθει προσκυνήσων εἰς Ἰερουσαλήμ,
verses.mistral: And rising up, he went; and behold, an Ethiopian eunuch, a court official of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, who was over all her treasure, had come to Jerusalem to worship.
verses.kjv: And he arose and went: and, behold, a man of Ethiopia, an eunuch of great authority under Candace queen of the Ethiopians, who had the charge of all her treasure, and had come to Jerusalem for to worship,

verses.greek: ἦν ⸀τε ὑποστρέφων καὶ καθήμενος ἐπὶ τοῦ ἅρματος αὐτοῦ καὶ ἀνεγίνωσκεν τὸν προφήτην Ἠσαΐαν.
verses.mistral: Reading the text, the English translation of Acts 8:28 is: "And he was going on his way and sitting, and he was reading the prophet Isaiah."
verses.kjv: Was returning, and sitting in his chariot read Esaias the prophet.

verses.greek: εἶπεν δὲ τὸ πνεῦμα τῷ Φιλίππῳ· Πρόσελθε καὶ κολλήθητι τῷ ἅρματι τούτῳ.
verses.mistral: Approach and join this chariot.
verses.kjv: Then the Spirit said unto Philip, Go near, and join thyself to this chariot.

verses.greek: προσδραμὼν δὲ ὁ Φίλιππος ἤκουσεν αὐτοῦ ἀναγινώσκοντος ⸂Ἠσαΐαν τὸν προφήτην⸃ καὶ εἶπεν· Ἆρά γε γινώσκεις ἃ ἀναγινώσκεις;
verses.mistral: Philip, hearing him read Isaiah the prophet, said: "Understandably, you are understanding what you are reading?"
verses.kjv: And Philip ran thither to him, and heard him read the prophet Esaias, and said, Understandest thou what thou readest?

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν· Πῶς γὰρ ἂν δυναίμην ἐὰν μή τις ⸀ὁδηγήσει με; παρεκάλεσέν τε τὸν Φίλιππον ἀναβάντα καθίσαι σὺν αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: He replied, "How can I, unless someone guides me?" He called Philip and said, "Come up and sit with me."
verses.kjv: And he said, How can I, except some man should guide me? And he desired Philip that he would come up and sit with him.

verses.greek: ἡ δὲ περιοχὴ τῆς γραφῆς ἣν ἀνεγίνωσκεν ἦν αὕτη· Ὡς πρόβατον ἐπὶ σφαγὴν ἤχθη, καὶ ὡς ἀμνὸς ἐναντίον τοῦ ⸀κείραντος αὐτὸν ἄφωνος, οὕτως οὐκ ἀνοίγει τὸ στόμα αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: The passage he was reading was this: "As a lamb led to the slaughter, and as a sheep before its shearers is silent, so he opens not his mouth."
verses.kjv: The place of the scripture which he read was this, He was led as a sheep to the slaughter; and like a lamb dumb before his shearer, so opened he not his mouth:

verses.greek: ἐν τῇ ⸀ταπεινώσει ἡ κρίσις αὐτοῦ ἤρθη· ⸀τὴν γενεὰν αὐτοῦ τίς διηγήσεται; ὅτι αἴρεται ἀπὸ τῆς γῆς ἡ ζωὴ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: In humility came his judgment; who will declare his generation? For the life of him was taken away from the earth.
verses.kjv: In his humiliation his judgment was taken away: and who shall declare his generation? for his life is taken from the earth.

verses.greek: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ εὐνοῦχος τῷ Φιλίππῳ εἶπεν· Δέομαί σου, περὶ τίνος ὁ προφήτης λέγει ⸀τοῦτο; περὶ ἑαυτοῦ ἢ περὶ ἑτέρου τινός;
verses.mistral: The eunuch answered Philip and said, "What is the prophet saying about him? Is it about himself or about some other?"
verses.kjv: And the eunuch answered Philip, and said, I pray thee, of whom speaketh the prophet this? of himself, or of some other man?

verses.greek: ἀνοίξας δὲ ὁ Φίλιππος τὸ στόμα αὐτοῦ καὶ ἀρξάμενος ἀπὸ τῆς γραφῆς ταύτης εὐηγγελίσατο αὐτῷ τὸν Ἰησοῦν.
verses.mistral: Philip opened his mouth and began, from this scripture, to proclaim Jesus to him.
verses.kjv: Then Philip opened his mouth, and began at the same scripture, and preached unto him Jesus.

verses.greek: ὡς δὲ ἐπορεύοντο κατὰ τὴν ὁδόν, ἦλθον ἐπί τι ὕδωρ, καί φησιν ὁ εὐνοῦχος· Ἰδοὺ ὕδωρ, τί κωλύει με ⸀βαπτισθῆναι;
verses.mistral: Here's the English translation of Acts 8:36, based on the Koine Greek text: "And as they were going along the road, they came upon some water, and the eunuch said, "Look! Water! What prevents me from being baptized?""
verses.kjv: And as they went on their way, they came unto a certain water: and the eunuch said, See, here is water; what doth hinder me to be baptized?

verses.greek: καὶ ἐκέλευσεν στῆναι τὸ ἅρμα, καὶ κατέβησαν ἀμφότεροι εἰς τὸ ὕδωρ ὅ τε Φίλιππος καὶ ὁ εὐνοῦχος, καὶ ἐβάπτισεν αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: And Philip baptized him
verses.kjv: And he commanded the chariot to stand still: and they went down both into the water, both Philip and the eunuch; and he baptized him.

verses.greek: ὅτε δὲ ἀνέβησαν ἐκ τοῦ ὕδατος, πνεῦμα κυρίου ἥρπασεν τὸν Φίλιππον, καὶ οὐκ εἶδεν αὐτὸν οὐκέτι ὁ εὐνοῦχος, ἐπορεύετο γὰρ τὴν ὁδὸν αὐτοῦ χαίρων.
verses.mistral: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord snatched Philip away, and the eunuch did not see him anymore; for he was going on his way rejoicing.
verses.kjv: And when they were come up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord caught away Philip, that the eunuch saw him no more: and he went on his way rejoicing.

verses.greek: Φίλιππος δὲ εὑρέθη εἰς Ἄζωτον, καὶ διερχόμενος εὐηγγελίζετο τὰς πόλεις πάσας ἕως τοῦ ἐλθεῖν αὐτὸν εἰς Καισάρειαν.
verses.mistral: Philip was found at Azotus, and as he passed through, he proclaimed the good news in all the cities until he came to Caesarea.
verses.kjv: But Philip was found at Azotus: and passing through he preached in all the cities, till he came to Caesarea.

verses.greek: Ὁ δὲ Σαῦλος ἔτι ἐμπνέων ἀπειλῆς καὶ φόνου εἰς τοὺς μαθητὰς τοῦ κυρίου, προσελθὼν τῷ ἀρχιερεῖ
verses.mistral: The Samuel, still breathing threats and murder against the disciples of the Lord, approached the high priest.
verses.kjv: And Saul, yet breathing out threatenings and slaughter against the disciples of the Lord, went unto the high priest,

verses.greek: ᾐτήσατο παρ’ αὐτοῦ ἐπιστολὰς εἰς Δαμασκὸν πρὸς τὰς συναγωγάς, ὅπως ἐάν τινας εὕρῃ τῆς ὁδοῦ ὄντας, ἄνδρας τε καὶ γυναῖκας, δεδεμένους ἀγάγῃ εἰς Ἰερουσαλήμ.
verses.mistral: He asked for letters from him to Damascus, to the synagogues, so that if he found any of the Way (people), whether men or women, he might bring them bound to Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: And desired of him letters to Damascus to the synagogues, that if he found any of this way, whether they were men or women, he might bring them bound unto Jerusalem.

verses.greek: ἐν δὲ τῷ πορεύεσθαι ἐγένετο αὐτὸν ἐγγίζειν τῇ Δαμασκῷ, ⸂ἐξαίφνης τε αὐτὸν περιήστραψεν⸃ φῶς ⸀ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ,
verses.mistral: En route to Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven shone around him.
verses.kjv: And as he journeyed, he came near Damascus: and suddenly there shined round about him a light from heaven:

verses.greek: καὶ πεσὼν ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν ἤκουσεν φωνὴν λέγουσαν αὐτῷ Σαοὺλ Σαούλ, τί με διώκεις;
verses.mistral: Fell to the ground, he heard a voice saying to him: "Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?"
verses.kjv: And he fell to the earth, and heard a voice saying unto him, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?

verses.greek: εἶπεν δέ· Τίς εἶ, κύριε; ὁ ⸀δέ· Ἐγώ εἰμι Ἰησοῦς ὃν σὺ διώκεις·
verses.mistral: I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting.
verses.kjv: And he said, Who art thou, Lord? And the Lord said, I am Jesus whom thou persecutest: it is hard for thee to kick against the pricks.

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ ἀνάστηθι καὶ εἴσελθε εἰς τὴν πόλιν, καὶ λαληθήσεταί σοι ⸂ὅ τί⸃ σε δεῖ ποιεῖν.
verses.mistral: Rise and go into the city, and it will be told to you what you must do.
verses.kjv: And he trembling and astonished said, Lord, what wilt thou have me to do? And the Lord said unto him, Arise, and go into the city, and it shall be told thee what thou must do.

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ ἄνδρες οἱ συνοδεύοντες αὐτῷ εἱστήκεισαν ἐνεοί, ἀκούοντες μὲν τῆς φωνῆς μηδένα δὲ θεωροῦντες.
verses.mistral: The men who were accompanying him stood speechless, hearing but seeing nothing.
verses.kjv: And the men which journeyed with him stood speechless, hearing a voice, but seeing no man.

verses.greek: ἠγέρθη δὲ ⸀Σαῦλος ἀπὸ τῆς γῆς, ἀνεῳγμένων ⸀δὲ τῶν ὀφθαλμῶν αὐτοῦ ⸀οὐδὲν ἔβλεπεν· χειραγωγοῦντες δὲ αὐτὸν εἰσήγαγον εἰς Δαμασκόν.
verses.mistral: Saul arose from the ground, and though his eyes were open, he saw nothing; they led him to Damascus.
verses.kjv: And Saul arose from the earth; and when his eyes were opened, he saw no man: but they led him by the hand, and brought him into Damascus.

verses.greek: καὶ ἦν ἡμέρας τρεῖς μὴ βλέπων, καὶ οὐκ ἔφαγεν οὐδὲ ἔπιεν.
verses.mistral: Three days without seeing, and neither did he eat or drink.
verses.kjv: And he was three days without sight, and neither did eat nor drink.

verses.greek: Ἦν δέ τις μαθητὴς ἐν Δαμασκῷ ὀνόματι Ἁνανίας, καὶ εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτὸν ⸂ἐν ὁράματι ὁ κύριος⸃· Ἁνανία. ὁ δὲ εἶπεν· Ἰδοὺ ἐγώ, κύριε.
verses.mistral: The disciple in Damascus was named Ananias, and the Lord said to him (in a vision). Ananias. And he replied, "Here I am, Lord."
verses.kjv: ¶ And there was a certain disciple at Damascus, named Ananias; and to him said the Lord in a vision, Ananias. And he said, Behold, I am here, Lord.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ κύριος πρὸς αὐτόν· ⸀Ἀναστὰς πορεύθητι ἐπὶ τὴν ῥύμην τὴν καλουμένην Εὐθεῖαν καὶ ζήτησον ἐν οἰκίᾳ Ἰούδα Σαῦλον ὀνόματι Ταρσέα, ἰδοὺ γὰρ προσεύχεται,
verses.mistral: Rise and go to the street called Straight, and seek in the house of Judas for Saul of Tarsus, for behold, he is praying.
verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto him, Arise, and go into the street which is called Straight, and enquire in the house of Judas for one called Saul, of Tarsus: for, behold, he prayeth,

verses.greek: καὶ εἶδεν ⸂ἄνδρα ἐν ὁράματι Ἁνανίαν ὀνόματι⸃ εἰσελθόντα καὶ ἐπιθέντα αὐτῷ ⸀χεῖρας ὅπως ἀναβλέψῃ.
verses.mistral: And he saw a man named Ananias entering and laying his hands on him, so that he might regain sight.
verses.kjv: And hath seen in a vision a man named Ananias coming in, and putting his hand on him, that he might receive his sight.

verses.greek: ἀπεκρίθη δὲ Ἁνανίας· Κύριε, ⸀ἤκουσα ἀπὸ πολλῶν περὶ τοῦ ἀνδρὸς τούτου, ὅσα κακὰ ⸂τοῖς ἁγίοις σου ἐποίησεν⸃ ἐν Ἰερουσαλήμ·
verses.mistral: Ananias replied, "Lord, I have heard from many about this man and the harm he has done to your holy ones in Jerusalem."
verses.kjv: Then Ananias answered, Lord, I have heard by many of this man, how much evil he hath done to thy saints at Jerusalem:

verses.greek: καὶ ὧδε ἔχει ἐξουσίαν παρὰ τῶν ἀρχιερέων δῆσαι πάντας τοὺς ἐπικαλουμένους τὸ ὄνομά σου.
verses.mistral: And here he has authority from the chief priests to arrest all who invoke Your name.
verses.kjv: And here he hath authority from the chief priests to bind all that call on thy name.

verses.greek: εἶπεν δὲ πρὸς αὐτὸν ὁ κύριος· Πορεύου, ὅτι σκεῦος ἐκλογῆς ⸂ἐστίν μοι⸃ οὗτος τοῦ βαστάσαι τὸ ὄνομά μου ἐνώπιον ⸀ἐθνῶν ⸀τε καὶ βασιλέων υἱῶν τε Ἰσραήλ,
verses.mistral: Said to him the Lord, "Go, for he is my chosen vessel to bear my name before nations and kings of Israel's sons."
verses.kjv: But the Lord said unto him, Go thy way: for he is a chosen vessel unto me, to bear my name before the Gentiles, and kings, and the children of Israel:

verses.greek: ἐγὼ γὰρ ὑποδείξω αὐτῷ ὅσα δεῖ αὐτὸν ὑπὲρ τοῦ ὀνόματός μου παθεῖν.
verses.mistral: I will show him what he must suffer on behalf of my name.
verses.kjv: For I will shew him how great things he must suffer for my name's sake.

verses.greek: ἀπῆλθεν δὲ Ἁνανίας καὶ εἰσῆλθεν εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν, καὶ ἐπιθεὶς ἐπ’ αὐτὸν τὰς χεῖρας εἶπεν· Σαοὺλ ἀδελφέ, ὁ κύριος ἀπέσταλκέν με, ⸀Ἰησοῦς ὁ ὀφθείς σοι ἐν τῇ ὁδῷ ᾗ ἤρχου, ὅπως ἀναβλέψῃς καὶ πλησθῇς πνεύματος ἁγίου.
verses.mistral: Hananiah departed and entered the house, and laying his hands on him said, "Saul, brother, the Lord has sent me, Jesus who appeared to you on the way in which you journeyed, so that you may look upon Him and receive the Holy Spirit."
verses.kjv: And Ananias went his way, and entered into the house; and putting his hands on him said, Brother Saul, the Lord, even Jesus, that appeared unto thee in the way as thou camest, hath sent me, that thou mightest receive thy sight, and be filled with the Holy Ghost.

verses.greek: καὶ εὐθέως ἀπέπεσαν ⸂αὐτοῦ ἀπὸ τῶν ὀφθαλμῶν⸃ ⸀ὡς λεπίδες, ἀνέβλεψέν τε καὶ ἀναστὰς ἐβαπτίσθη,
verses.mistral: Immediately, from his eyes fell to the ground like scales, and he also looked up and stood, and was baptized.
verses.kjv: And immediately there fell from his eyes as it had been scales: and he received sight forthwith, and arose, and was baptized.

verses.greek: καὶ λαβὼν τροφὴν ⸀ἐνίσχυσεν. Ἐγένετο ⸀δὲ μετὰ τῶν ἐν Δαμασκῷ μαθητῶν ἡμέρας τινὰς,
verses.mistral: And taking food, he was strengthened. After a few days with the disciples in Damascus.
verses.kjv: And when he had received meat, he was strengthened. Then was Saul certain days with the disciples which were at Damascus.

verses.greek: καὶ εὐθέως ἐν ταῖς συναγωγαῖς ἐκήρυσσεν τὸν ⸀Ἰησοῦν ὅτι οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: Immediately in the synagogues he began to proclaim Jesus, saying that He is the Son of God.
verses.kjv: And straightway he preached Christ in the synagogues, that he is the Son of God.

verses.greek: ἐξίσταντο δὲ πάντες οἱ ἀκούοντες καὶ ἔλεγον· Οὐχ οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ πορθήσας ⸀ἐν Ἰερουσαλὴμ τοὺς ἐπικαλουμένους τὸ ὄνομα τοῦτο, καὶ ὧδε εἰς τοῦτο ἐληλύθει ἵνα δεδεμένους αὐτοὺς ἀγάγῃ ἐπὶ τοὺς ἀρχιερεῖς;
verses.mistral: All who heard were astonished and said, "Is not this the one who caused chaos in Jerusalem by calling upon this name, and has come here to seize those bound for the high priests?"
verses.kjv: But all that heard him were amazed, and said; Is not this he that destroyed them which called on this name in Jerusalem, and came hither for that intent, that he might bring them bound unto the chief priests?

verses.greek: Σαῦλος δὲ μᾶλλον ἐνεδυναμοῦτο καὶ ⸀συνέχυννεν ⸀τοὺς Ἰουδαίους τοὺς κατοικοῦντας ἐν Δαμασκῷ, συμβιβάζων ὅτι οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ χριστός.
verses.mistral: Samuel was more and more empowered, and he strengthened the Jews who lived in Damascus, confirming to them that this is the Christ.
verses.kjv: But Saul increased the more in strength, and confounded the Jews which dwelt at Damascus, proving that this is very Christ.

verses.greek: Ὡς δὲ ἐπληροῦντο ἡμέραι ἱκαναί, συνεβουλεύσαντο οἱ Ἰουδαῖοι ἀνελεῖν αὐτόν·
verses.mistral: Plotted the Jews to seize him
verses.kjv: ¶ And after that many days were fulfilled, the Jews took counsel to kill him:

verses.greek: ἐγνώσθη δὲ τῷ Σαύλῳ ἡ ἐπιβουλὴ αὐτῶν. ⸂παρετηροῦντο δὲ καὶ⸃ τὰς πύλας ἡμέρας τε καὶ νυκτὸς ὅπως αὐτὸν ἀνέλωσιν·
verses.mistral: The plot of theirs was known to Paul. They were also watching the gates day and night, so as to seize him.
verses.kjv: But their laying await was known of Saul. And they watched the gates day and night to kill him.

verses.greek: λαβόντες δὲ ⸂οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ⸃ νυκτὸς ⸂διὰ τοῦ τείχους καθῆκαν αὐτὸν⸃ χαλάσαντες ἐν σπυρίδι.
verses.mistral: Seizing him by night through the walls, they lowered him in a basket.
verses.kjv: Then the disciples took him by night, and let him down by the wall in a basket.

verses.greek: Παραγενόμενος δὲ ⸀εἰς Ἰερουσαλὴμ ⸀ἐπείραζεν κολλᾶσθαι τοῖς μαθηταῖς· καὶ πάντες ἐφοβοῦντο αὐτόν, μὴ πιστεύοντες ὅτι ἐστὶν μαθητής.
verses.mistral: Arriving in Jerusalem, he attempted to join the disciples; and they all feared him, not believing that he was a disciple.
verses.kjv: And when Saul was come to Jerusalem, he assayed to join himself to the disciples: but they were all afraid of him, and believed not that he was a disciple.

verses.greek: Βαρναβᾶς δὲ ἐπιλαβόμενος αὐτὸν ἤγαγεν πρὸς τοὺς ἀποστόλους, καὶ διηγήσατο αὐτοῖς πῶς ἐν τῇ ὁδῷ εἶδεν τὸν κύριον καὶ ὅτι ἐλάλησεν αὐτῷ καὶ πῶς ἐν Δαμασκῷ ἐπαρρησιάσατο ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι ⸀τοῦ Ἰησοῦ.
verses.mistral: Barnabas took him to the apostles, and recounted to them how on the journey he had seen the Lord, and that He spoke to him, and how in Damascus he had spoken boldly in the name of Jesus.
verses.kjv: But Barnabas took him, and brought him to the apostles, and declared unto them how he had seen the Lord in the way, and that he had spoken to him, and how he had preached boldly at Damascus in the name of Jesus.

verses.greek: καὶ ἦν μετ’ αὐτῶν εἰσπορευόμενος ⸂καὶ ἐκπορευόμενος⸃ εἰς ⸀Ἰερουσαλήμ, παρρησιαζόμενος ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι τοῦ ⸀κυρίου,
verses.mistral: And was with them going to Jerusalem, speaking boldly in the name of the Lord.
verses.kjv: And he was with them coming in and going out at Jerusalem.

verses.greek: ἐλάλει τε καὶ συνεζήτει πρὸς τοὺς Ἑλληνιστάς· οἱ δὲ ἐπεχείρουν ⸂ἀνελεῖν αὐτόν⸃.
verses.mistral: Speaks also and argues with the Greeks; however, they attempt to kill him.
verses.kjv: And he spake boldly in the name of the Lord Jesus, and disputed against the Grecians: but they went about to slay him.

verses.greek: ἐπιγνόντες δὲ οἱ ἀδελφοὶ κατήγαγον αὐτὸν εἰς Καισάρειαν καὶ ἐξαπέστειλαν αὐτὸν εἰς Ταρσόν.
verses.mistral: Brothers knowing, brought him to Caesarea and sent him to Tarsus.
verses.kjv: Which when the brethren knew, they brought him down to Caesarea, and sent him forth to Tarsus.

verses.greek: ⸂Ἡ μὲν οὖν ἐκκλησία καθ’ ὅλης τῆς Ἰουδαίας καὶ Γαλιλαίας καὶ Σαμαρείας εἶχεν εἰρήνην οἰκοδομουμένη, καὶ πορευομένη τῷ φόβῳ τοῦ κυρίου καὶ τῇ παρακλήσει τοῦ ἁγίου πνεύματος ἐπληθύνετο⸃.
verses.mistral: The church throughout all Judea, Galilee, and Samaria enjoyed peace and was being built up; it was increasing in number as the fear of the Lord and the encouragement of the holy Spirit came upon it.
verses.kjv: Then had the churches rest throughout all Judaea and Galilee and Samaria, and were edified; and walking in the fear of the Lord, and in the comfort of the Holy Ghost, were multiplied.

verses.greek: Ἐγένετο δὲ Πέτρον διερχόμενον διὰ πάντων κατελθεῖν καὶ πρὸς τοὺς ἁγίους τοὺς κατοικοῦντας Λύδδα.
verses.mistral: Arrived Peter through Lydda.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, as Peter passed throughout all quarters, he came down also to the saints which dwelt at Lydda.

verses.greek: εὗρεν δὲ ἐκεῖ ἄνθρωπόν τινα ⸂ὀνόματι Αἰνέαν⸃ ἐξ ἐτῶν ὀκτὼ κατακείμενον ἐπὶ ⸀κραβάττου, ὃς ἦν παραλελυμένος.
verses.mistral: Found there a certain man named Aeneas, bedridden for eight years, who was paralyzed.
verses.kjv: And there he found a certain man named Aeneas, which had kept his bed eight years, and was sick of the palsy.

verses.greek: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ὁ Πέτρος· Αἰνέα, ἰᾶταί σε Ἰησοῦς ⸀Χριστός· ἀνάστηθι καὶ στρῶσον σεαυτῷ· καὶ εὐθέως ἀνέστη.
verses.mistral: Jesus Christ heals you, Aeneas; rise up and make your bed. And immediately he rose up.
verses.kjv: And Peter said unto him, Aeneas, Jesus Christ maketh thee whole: arise, and make thy bed. And he arose immediately.

verses.greek: καὶ εἶδαν αὐτὸν πάντες οἱ κατοικοῦντες Λύδδα καὶ τὸν ⸀Σαρῶνα, οἵτινες ἐπέστρεψαν ἐπὶ τὸν κύριον.
verses.mistral: All the residents of Lydda saw him, and Sarona also returned to the Lord.
verses.kjv: And all that dwelt at Lydda and Saron saw him, and turned to the Lord.

verses.greek: Ἐν Ἰόππῃ δέ τις ἦν μαθήτρια ὀνόματι Ταβιθά, ἣ διερμηνευομένη λέγεται Δορκάς· αὕτη ἦν πλήρης ⸂ἔργων ἀγαθῶν⸃ καὶ ἐλεημοσυνῶν ὧν ἐποίει.
verses.mistral: This woman in Joppa was named Tabitha, also called Dorcas; she was full of good deeds and acts of kindness that she performed.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now there was at Joppa a certain disciple named Tabitha, which by interpretation is called Dorcas: this woman was full of good works and almsdeeds which she did.

verses.greek: ἐγένετο δὲ ἐν ταῖς ἡμέραις ἐκείναις ἀσθενήσασαν αὐτὴν ἀποθανεῖν· λούσαντες δὲ ⸂ἔθηκαν αὐτὴν⸃ ἐν ὑπερῴῳ.
verses.mistral: She fell ill and died in those days; after they had washed her, they laid her out in an upper room.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass in those days, that she was sick, and died: whom when they had washed, they laid her in an upper chamber.

verses.greek: ἐγγὺς δὲ οὔσης Λύδδας τῇ Ἰόππῃ οἱ μαθηταὶ ἀκούσαντες ὅτι Πέτρος ἐστὶν ἐν αὐτῇ ἀπέστειλαν ⸂δύο ἄνδρας⸃ πρὸς αὐτὸν παρακαλοῦντες· Μὴ ⸀ὀκνήσῃς διελθεῖν ἕως ⸀ἡμῶν·
verses.mistral: Approach her, for Peter is in her. Sending two men to him, they urged him not to hesitate until us.
verses.kjv: And forasmuch as Lydda was nigh to Joppa, and the disciples had heard that Peter was there, they sent unto him two men, desiring him that he would not delay to come to them.

verses.greek: ἀναστὰς δὲ Πέτρος συνῆλθεν αὐτοῖς· ὃν παραγενόμενον ἀνήγαγον εἰς τὸ ὑπερῷον, καὶ παρέστησαν αὐτῷ πᾶσαι αἱ χῆραι κλαίουσαι καὶ ἐπιδεικνύμεναι χιτῶνας καὶ ἱμάτια ὅσα ἐποίει μετ’ αὐτῶν οὖσα ἡ Δορκάς.
verses.mistral: Peter rose and went with them. When he had come up, they led him into the upper room, where all the widows stood weeping and displaying the garments and clothing that Dorcas had made while she was with them.
verses.kjv: Then Peter arose and went with them. When he was come, they brought him into the upper chamber: and all the widows stood by him weeping, and shewing the coats and garments which Dorcas made, while she was with them.

verses.greek: ἐκβαλὼν δὲ ἔξω πάντας ὁ Πέτρος ⸀καὶ θεὶς τὰ γόνατα προσηύξατο, καὶ ἐπιστρέψας πρὸς τὸ σῶμα εἶπεν· Ταβιθά, ἀνάστηθι. ἡ δὲ ἤνοιξεν τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς αὐτῆς, καὶ ἰδοῦσα τὸν Πέτρον ἀνεκάθισεν.
verses.mistral: Peter, casting out all, knelt down and prayed, and turning to the body said, "Tabitha, arise." And she opened her eyes, and seeing Peter, sat up.
verses.kjv: But Peter put them all forth, and kneeled down, and prayed; and turning him to the body said, Tabitha, arise. And she opened her eyes: and when she saw Peter, she sat up.

verses.greek: δοὺς δὲ αὐτῇ χεῖρα ἀνέστησεν αὐτήν, φωνήσας δὲ τοὺς ἁγίους καὶ τὰς χήρας παρέστησεν αὐτὴν ζῶσαν.
verses.mistral: Gives her hand, raising her up was she; calling to the saints and widows, presented her living.
verses.kjv: And he gave her his hand, and lifted her up, and when he had called the saints and widows, presented her alive.

verses.greek: γνωστὸν δὲ ἐγένετο καθ’ ὅλης ⸀τῆς Ἰόππης, καὶ ⸂ἐπίστευσαν πολλοὶ⸃ ἐπὶ τὸν κύριον.
verses.mistral: Known therefore throughout all Joppa, and many believed on the Lord.
verses.kjv: And it was known throughout all Joppa; and many believed in the Lord.

verses.greek: ἐγένετο ⸀δὲ ἡμέρας ἱκανὰς ⸀μεῖναι ἐν Ἰόππῃ παρά τινι Σίμωνι βυρσεῖ.
verses.mistral: Stayed many days in Joppa with Simon the Tanner.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that he tarried many days in Joppa with one Simon a tanner.

verses.greek: Ἀνὴρ δέ ⸀τις ἐν Καισαρείᾳ ὀνόματι Κορνήλιος, ἑκατοντάρχης ἐκ σπείρης τῆς καλουμένης Ἰταλικῆς,
verses.mistral: Man in Caesarea named Cornelius, centurion of the cohort called Italian.
verses.kjv: There was a certain man in Caesarea called Cornelius, a centurion of the band called the Italian band,

verses.greek: εὐσεβὴς καὶ φοβούμενος τὸν θεὸν σὺν παντὶ τῷ οἴκῳ αὐτοῦ, ⸀ποιῶν ἐλεημοσύνας πολλὰς τῷ λαῷ καὶ δεόμενος τοῦ θεοῦ διὰ παντός,
verses.mistral: God-fearing and reverent, Cornelius was, together with his entire household; performing much charity towards the people and continually seeking God.
verses.kjv: A devout man, and one that feared God with all his house, which gave much alms to the people, and prayed to God alway.

verses.greek: εἶδεν ἐν ὁράματι φανερῶς ὡσεὶ ⸀περὶ ὥραν ἐνάτην τῆς ἡμέρας ἄγγελον τοῦ θεοῦ εἰσελθόντα πρὸς αὐτὸν καὶ εἰπόντα αὐτῷ· Κορνήλιε.
verses.mistral: Saw in a vision clearly about the ninth hour of the day an angel of God entering and saying to him, "Cornelius."
verses.kjv: He saw in a vision evidently about the ninth hour of the day an angel of God coming in to him, and saying unto him, Cornelius.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀτενίσας αὐτῷ καὶ ἔμφοβος γενόμενος εἶπεν· Τί ἐστιν, κύριε; εἶπεν δὲ αὐτῷ· Αἱ προσευχαί σου καὶ αἱ ἐλεημοσύναι σου ἀνέβησαν εἰς μνημόσυνον ⸀ἔμπροσθεν τοῦ θεοῦ·
verses.mistral: He asked, "What is it, Lord?" And he said to him, "Your prayers and your alms have ascended as a memorial before God."
verses.kjv: And when he looked on him, he was afraid, and said, What is it, Lord? And he said unto him, Thy prayers and thine alms are come up for a memorial before God.

verses.greek: καὶ νῦν πέμψον ⸂ἄνδρας εἰς Ἰόππην⸃ καὶ μετάπεμψαι Σίμωνά ⸂τινα ὃς ἐπικαλεῖται Πέτρος⸃·
verses.mistral: Send men to Joppa and send Simon, who is called Peter.
verses.kjv: And now send men to Joppa, and call for one Simon, whose surname is Peter:

verses.greek: οὗτος ξενίζεται παρά τινι Σίμωνι βυρσεῖ, ᾧ ἐστιν οἰκία παρὰ θάλασσαν.
verses.mistral: This lodges with Simon the tanner, who is by the sea.
verses.kjv: He lodgeth with one Simon a tanner, whose house is by the sea side: he shall tell thee what thou oughtest to do.

verses.greek: ὡς δὲ ἀπῆλθεν ὁ ἄγγελος ὁ λαλῶν ⸀αὐτῷ, φωνήσας δύο τῶν ⸀οἰκετῶν καὶ στρατιώτην εὐσεβῆ τῶν προσκαρτερούντων αὐτῷ
verses.mistral: As the angel who had been speaking to him went his way, he called out, addressing two of his servants and a devout soldier named Cornelius.
verses.kjv: And when the angel which spake unto Cornelius was departed, he called two of his household servants, and a devout soldier of them that waited on him continually;

verses.greek: καὶ ἐξηγησάμενος ⸂ἅπαντα αὐτοῖς⸃ ἀπέστειλεν αὐτοὺς εἰς τὴν Ἰόππην.
verses.mistral: Sent them to Joppa.
verses.kjv: And when he had declared all these things unto them, he sent them to Joppa.

verses.greek: Τῇ δὲ ἐπαύριον ὁδοιπορούντων ἐκείνων καὶ τῇ πόλει ἐγγιζόντων ἀνέβη Πέτρος ἐπὶ τὸ δῶμα προσεύξασθαι περὶ ὥραν ἕκτην.
verses.mistral: The next day, as they were journeying and approaching the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray at noon.
verses.kjv: ¶ On the morrow, as they went on their journey, and drew nigh unto the city, Peter went up upon the housetop to pray about the sixth hour:

verses.greek: ἐγένετο δὲ πρόσπεινος καὶ ἤθελεν γεύσασθαι· παρασκευαζόντων δὲ ⸀αὐτῶν ⸀ἐγένετο ἐπ’ αὐτὸν ἔκστασις,
verses.mistral: Suddenly, Peter became hungry and wanted to eat. As they were preparing it, a trance came upon him.
verses.kjv: And he became very hungry, and would have eaten: but while they made ready, he fell into a trance,

verses.greek: καὶ θεωρεῖ τὸν οὐρανὸν ἀνεῳγμένον καὶ ⸀καταβαῖνον σκεῦός τι ὡς ὀθόνην μεγάλην τέσσαρσιν ⸀ἀρχαῖς καθιέμενον ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς,
verses.mistral: Opening heaven, Peter saw something like a large sheet coming down, being lowered by its four corners to the earth.
verses.kjv: And saw heaven opened, and a certain vessel descending unto him, as it had been a great sheet knit at the four corners, and let down to the earth:

verses.greek: ἐν ᾧ ὑπῆρχεν πάντα τὰ τετράποδα ⸂καὶ ἑρπετὰ τῆς γῆς καὶ⸃ πετεινὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ.
verses.mistral: All quadrupeds, creatures that creep on the ground, and flying birds from heaven.
verses.kjv: Wherein were all manner of fourfooted beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and fowls of the air.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐγένετο φωνὴ πρὸς αὐτόν· Ἀναστάς, Πέτρε, θῦσον καὶ φάγε.
verses.mistral: Rise, Peter, kill and eat.
verses.kjv: And there came a voice to him, Rise, Peter; kill, and eat.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Πέτρος εἶπεν· Μηδαμῶς, κύριε, ὅτι οὐδέποτε ἔφαγον πᾶν κοινὸν ⸀καὶ ἀκάθαρτον.
verses.mistral: Peter said: Never, Lord, for I have never eaten anything unclean or common.
verses.kjv: But Peter said, Not so, Lord; for I have never eaten any thing that is common or unclean.

verses.greek: καὶ φωνὴ πάλιν ἐκ δευτέρου πρὸς αὐτόν· Ἃ ὁ θεὸς ἐκαθάρισεν σὺ μὴ κοίνου.
verses.mistral: Clean, that which God has declared clean.
verses.kjv: And the voice spake unto him again the second time, What God hath cleansed, that call not thou common.

verses.greek: τοῦτο δὲ ἐγένετο ἐπὶ τρίς, καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἀνελήμφθη τὸ σκεῦος εἰς τὸν οὐρανόν.
verses.mistral: This occurred three times, and immediately the vessel was taken up into heaven.
verses.kjv: This was done thrice: and the vessel was received up again into heaven.

verses.greek: Ὡς δὲ ἐν ἑαυτῷ διηπόρει ὁ Πέτρος τί ἂν εἴη τὸ ὅραμα ὃ εἶδεν, ⸀ἰδοὺ οἱ ἄνδρες οἱ ἀπεσταλμένοι ⸀ὑπὸ τοῦ Κορνηλίου διερωτήσαντες τὴν οἰκίαν ⸀τοῦ Σίμωνος ἐπέστησαν ἐπὶ τὸν πυλῶνα,
verses.mistral: As Peter wondered within himself what the vision he saw could mean, indeed the men who were sent by Cornelius asked for the house of Simon and stood at the gate.
verses.kjv: Now while Peter doubted in himself what this vision which he had seen should mean, behold, the men which were sent from Cornelius had made enquiry for Simon's house, and stood before the gate,

verses.greek: καὶ φωνήσαντες ⸀ἐπυνθάνοντο εἰ Σίμων ὁ ἐπικαλούμενος Πέτρος ἐνθάδε ξενίζεται.
verses.mistral: And they inquired if Simon called Peter was dwelling here.
verses.kjv: And called, and asked whether Simon, which was surnamed Peter, were lodged there.

verses.greek: τοῦ δὲ Πέτρου διενθυμουμένου περὶ τοῦ ὁράματος εἶπεν ⸂αὐτῷ τὸ πνεῦμα⸃· Ἰδοὺ ⸀ἄνδρες ⸀ζητοῦντές σε·
verses.mistral: The Spirit said to him, "Behold, men are seeking you."
verses.kjv: ¶ While Peter thought on the vision, the Spirit said unto him, Behold, three men seek thee.

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ ἀναστὰς κατάβηθι καὶ πορεύου σὺν αὐτοῖς μηδὲν διακρινόμενος, ⸀ὅτι ἐγὼ ἀπέσταλκα αὐτούς.
verses.mistral: Go with them, and do not hesitate, for I have sent them.
verses.kjv: Arise therefore, and get thee down, and go with them, doubting nothing: for I have sent them.

verses.greek: καταβὰς δὲ Πέτρος πρὸς τοὺς ἄνδρας εἶπεν· Ἰδοὺ ἐγώ εἰμι ὃν ζητεῖτε· τίς ἡ αἰτία δι’ ἣν πάρεστε;
verses.mistral: Peter went down with them, and said: "Behold, I am he whom you seek; why are you here?"
verses.kjv: Then Peter went down to the men which were sent unto him from Cornelius; and said, Behold, I am he whom ye seek: what is the cause wherefore ye are come?

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ εἶπαν· Κορνήλιος ἑκατοντάρχης, ἀνὴρ δίκαιος καὶ φοβούμενος τὸν θεὸν μαρτυρούμενός τε ὑπὸ ὅλου τοῦ ἔθνους τῶν Ἰουδαίων, ἐχρηματίσθη ὑπὸ ἀγγέλου ἁγίου μεταπέμψασθαί σε εἰς τὸν οἶκον αὐτοῦ καὶ ἀκοῦσαι ῥήματα παρὰ σοῦ.
verses.mistral: Cornelius, a centurion, a devout man and one who feared God with all his household, has been directed by a holy angel to send for you and hear words from you.
verses.kjv: And they said, Cornelius the centurion, a just man, and one that feareth God, and of good report among all the nation of the Jews, was warned from God by an holy angel to send for thee into his house, and to hear words of thee.

verses.greek: εἰσκαλεσάμενος οὖν αὐτοὺς ἐξένισεν. Τῇ δὲ ἐπαύριον ⸀ἀναστὰς ἐξῆλθεν σὺν αὐτοῖς, καί τινες τῶν ἀδελφῶν τῶν ἀπὸ Ἰόππης συνῆλθον αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: Called them therefore he lodged with them, and on the next day rising set out with them, some of the brethren from Ippos accompanied him.
verses.kjv: Then called he them in, and lodged them. And on the morrow Peter went away with them, and certain brethren from Joppa accompanied him.

verses.greek: ⸂τῇ δὲ⸃ ἐπαύριον ⸀εἰσῆλθεν εἰς τὴν Καισάρειαν. ὁ δὲ Κορνήλιος ἦν προσδοκῶν αὐτοὺς συγκαλεσάμενος τοὺς συγγενεῖς αὐτοῦ καὶ τοὺς ἀναγκαίους φίλους.
verses.mistral: The following day he entered Caesarea. Cornelius was expecting them, having summoned his relatives and close friends.
verses.kjv: And the morrow after they entered into Caesarea. And Cornelius waited for them, and had called together his kinsmen and near friends.

verses.greek: ὡς δὲ ἐγένετο τοῦ εἰσελθεῖν τὸν Πέτρον, συναντήσας αὐτῷ ὁ Κορνήλιος πεσὼν ἐπὶ τοὺς πόδας προσεκύνησεν.
verses.mistral: As Peter entered, Cornelius fell at his feet and worshipped him.
verses.kjv: And as Peter was coming in, Cornelius met him, and fell down at his feet, and worshipped him.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Πέτρος ⸂ἤγειρεν αὐτὸν⸃ λέγων· Ἀνάστηθι· καὶ ἐγὼ αὐτὸς ἄνθρωπός εἰμι.
verses.mistral: Peter raised him up, saying, "Arise! I too am a man."
verses.kjv: But Peter took him up, saying, Stand up; I myself also am a man.

verses.greek: καὶ συνομιλῶν αὐτῷ εἰσῆλθεν, καὶ εὑρίσκει συνεληλυθότας πολλούς,
verses.mistral: Entered and found many gathered
verses.kjv: And as he talked with him, he went in, and found many that were come together.

verses.greek: ἔφη τε πρὸς αὐτούς· Ὑμεῖς ἐπίστασθε ὡς ἀθέμιτόν ἐστιν ἀνδρὶ Ἰουδαίῳ κολλᾶσθαι ἢ προσέρχεσθαι ἀλλοφύλῳ· κἀμοὶ ὁ θεὸς ἔδειξεν μηδένα κοινὸν ἢ ἀκάθαρτον λέγειν ἄνθρωπον·
verses.mistral: You spoke to them, saying, "You know it is unlawful for a Jewish man or to go to one of another nationality. Even I was shown by God that no person is common or unclean."
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Ye know how that it is an unlawful thing for a man that is a Jew to keep company, or come unto one of another nation; but God hath shewed me that I should not call any man common or unclean.

verses.greek: διὸ καὶ ἀναντιρρήτως ἦλθον μεταπεμφθείς πυνθάνομαι οὖν τίνι λόγῳ μετεπέμψασθέ με.
verses.mistral: Therefore I came also, having been sent, to learn whom among you I might proclaim the message.
verses.kjv: Therefore came I unto you without gainsaying, as soon as I was sent for: I ask therefore for what intent ye have sent for me?

verses.greek: Καὶ ὁ Κορνήλιος ἔφη· Ἀπὸ τετάρτης ἡμέρας μέχρι ταύτης τῆς ὥρας ⸀ἤμην τὴν ⸀ἐνάτην προσευχόμενος ἐν τῷ οἴκῳ μου, καὶ ἰδοὺ ἀνὴρ ἔστη ἐνώπιόν μου ἐν ἐσθῆτι λαμπρᾷ
verses.mistral: And Cornelius said, "From the fourth day's hour until this present hour, I have been praying in my house, and behold, a man stood before me in bright attire"
verses.kjv: And Cornelius said, Four days ago I was fasting until this hour; and at the ninth hour I prayed in my house, and, behold, a man stood before me in bright clothing,

verses.greek: καὶ φησί· Κορνήλιε, εἰσηκούσθη σου ἡ προσευχὴ καὶ αἱ ἐλεημοσύναι σου ἐμνήσθησαν ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: And he says, "Cornelius, your prayer has been heard and the compassion shown by you was remembered before God."
verses.kjv: And said, Cornelius, thy prayer is heard, and thine alms are had in remembrance in the sight of God.

verses.greek: πέμψον οὖν εἰς Ἰόππην καὶ μετακάλεσαι Σίμωνα ὃς ἐπικαλεῖται Πέτρος· οὗτος ξενίζεται ἐν οἰκίᾳ Σίμωνος βυρσέως παρὰ ⸀θάλασσαν.
verses.mistral: Send therefore to Joppa, and call for Simon who is called Peter. He lodges in the house of Simon the tanner by the sea.
verses.kjv: Send therefore to Joppa, and call hither Simon, whose surname is Peter; he is lodged in the house of one Simon a tanner by the sea side: who, when he cometh, shall speak unto thee.

verses.greek: ἐξαυτῆς οὖν ἔπεμψα πρὸς σέ, σύ τε καλῶς ἐποίησας παραγενόμενος. νῦν οὖν πάντες ἡμεῖς ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ πάρεσμεν ἀκοῦσαι πάντα τὰ προστεταγμένα σοι ὑπὸ τοῦ ⸀κυρίου.
verses.mistral: Immediately therefore I sent to thee, thou hast done well coming; now we all stand before God to hear all things commanded thee by the Lord.
verses.kjv: Immediately therefore I sent to thee; and thou hast well done that thou art come. Now therefore are we all here present before God, to hear all things that are commanded thee of God.

verses.greek: Ἀνοίξας δὲ Πέτρος τὸ στόμα εἶπεν· Ἐπ’ ἀληθείας καταλαμβάνομαι ὅτι οὐκ ἔστιν προσωπολήμπτης ὁ θεός,
verses.mistral: Opens Peter his mouth and said: "In truth I perceive that God is no respecter of persons"
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Peter opened his mouth, and said, Of a truth I perceive that God is no respecter of persons:

verses.greek: ἀλλ’ ἐν παντὶ ἔθνει ὁ φοβούμενος αὐτὸν καὶ ἐργαζόμενος δικαιοσύνην δεκτὸς αὐτῷ ἐστιν.
verses.mistral: All in every nation, he who fears Him and works righteousness is acceptable to Him.
verses.kjv: But in every nation he that feareth him, and worketh righteousness, is accepted with him.

verses.greek: τὸν λόγον ⸀ὃν ἀπέστειλεν τοῖς υἱοῖς Ἰσραὴλ εὐαγγελιζόμενος εἰρήνην διὰ Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ· οὗτός ἐστιν πάντων κύριος.
verses.mistral: This one is Lord of all. (Acts 10:36, referring to Jesus Christ)
verses.kjv: The word which God sent unto the children of Israel, preaching peace by Jesus Christ: (he is Lord of all:)

verses.greek: ὑμεῖς οἴδατε τὸ γενόμενον ῥῆμα καθ’ ὅλης τῆς Ἰουδαίας, ⸀ἀρξάμενος ἀπὸ τῆς Γαλιλαίας μετὰ τὸ βάπτισμα ὃ ἐκήρυξεν Ἰωάννης,
verses.mistral: You know the word that has spread throughout all Judea, starting from Galilee after John's baptism.
verses.kjv: That word, I say, ye know, which was published throughout all Judaea, and began from Galilee, after the baptism which John preached;

verses.greek: Ἰησοῦν τὸν ἀπὸ Ναζαρέθ, ὡς ἔχρισεν αὐτὸν ὁ θεὸς πνεύματι ἁγίῳ καὶ δυνάμει, ὃς διῆλθεν εὐεργετῶν καὶ ἰώμενος πάντας τοὺς καταδυναστευομένους ὑπὸ τοῦ διαβόλου, ὅτι ὁ θεὸς ἦν μετ’ αὐτοῦ·
verses.mistral: Jesus of Nazareth, whom God anointed with the Holy Spirit and power, He went about doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, for God was with him.
verses.kjv: How God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Ghost and with power: who went about doing good, and healing all that were oppressed of the devil; for God was with him.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἡμεῖς μάρτυρες πάντων ὧν ἐποίησεν ἔν τε τῇ χώρᾳ τῶν Ἰουδαίων ⸀καὶ Ἰερουσαλήμ· ὃν καὶ ἀνεῖλαν κρεμάσαντες ἐπὶ ξύλου.
verses.mistral: And we are witnesses to all the things he did, both in the land of the Jews and in Jerusalem; whom they also killed by hanging on a tree.
verses.kjv: And we are witnesses of all things which he did both in the land of the Jews, and in Jerusalem; whom they slew and hanged on a tree:

verses.greek: τοῦτον ὁ θεὸς ἤγειρεν ⸀τῇ τρίτῃ ἡμέρᾳ καὶ ἔδωκεν αὐτὸν ἐμφανῆ γενέσθαι,
verses.mistral: This man God raised on the third day and made visible.
verses.kjv: Him God raised up the third day, and shewed him openly;

verses.greek: οὐ παντὶ τῷ λαῷ ἀλλὰ μάρτυσι τοῖς προκεχειροτονημένοις ὑπὸ τοῦ θεοῦ, ἡμῖν, οἵτινες συνεφάγομεν καὶ συνεπίομεν αὐτῷ μετὰ τὸ ἀναστῆναι αὐτὸν ἐκ νεκρῶν·
verses.mistral: Believers, to the witnesses chosen by God: we who ate and drank with him after he arose from the dead.
verses.kjv: Not to all the people, but unto witnesses chosen before of God, even to us, who did eat and drink with him after he rose from the dead.

verses.greek: καὶ παρήγγειλεν ἡμῖν κηρύξαι τῷ λαῷ καὶ διαμαρτύρασθαι ὅτι ⸀οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ ὡρισμένος ὑπὸ τοῦ θεοῦ κριτὴς ζώντων καὶ νεκρῶν.
verses.mistral: And he commanded us to preach to the people and testify that this man is the one appointed by God as Judge of the living and the dead.
verses.kjv: And he commanded us to preach unto the people, and to testify that it is he which was ordained of God to be the Judge of quick and dead.

verses.greek: τούτῳ πάντες οἱ προφῆται μαρτυροῦσιν, ἄφεσιν ἁμαρτιῶν λαβεῖν διὰ τοῦ ὀνόματος αὐτοῦ πάντα τὸν πιστεύοντα εἰς αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: All prophets testify concerning him that through his name everyone who believes in him will receive forgiveness of sins.
verses.kjv: To him give all the prophets witness, that through his name whosoever believeth in him shall receive remission of sins.

verses.greek: Ἔτι λαλοῦντος τοῦ Πέτρου τὰ ῥήματα ταῦτα ἐπέπεσε τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον ἐπὶ πάντας τοὺς ἀκούοντας τὸν λόγον.
verses.mistral: The Holy Spirit fell upon all who were listening to the word.
verses.kjv: ¶ While Peter yet spake these words, the Holy Ghost fell on all them which heard the word.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐξέστησαν οἱ ἐκ περιτομῆς πιστοὶ ⸀ὅσοι συνῆλθαν τῷ Πέτρῳ, ὅτι καὶ ἐπὶ τὰ ἔθνη ἡ δωρεὰ τοῦ ⸂ἁγίου πνεύματος⸃ ἐκκέχυται·
verses.mistral: And the circumcision believers who had come with Peter, realized that even to the Gentiles the gift of the Holy Spirit was poured out.
verses.kjv: And they of the circumcision which believed were astonished, as many as came with Peter, because that on the Gentiles also was poured out the gift of the Holy Ghost.

verses.greek: ἤκουον γὰρ αὐτῶν λαλούντων γλώσσαις καὶ μεγαλυνόντων τὸν θεόν. τότε ⸀ἀπεκρίθη Πέτρος·
verses.mistral: They heard them speaking in tongues and magnifying God. Then Peter replied.
verses.kjv: For they heard them speak with tongues, and magnify God. Then answered Peter,

verses.greek: Μήτι τὸ ὕδωρ ⸂δύναται κωλῦσαί⸃ τις τοῦ μὴ βαπτισθῆναι τούτους οἵτινες τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον ἔλαβον ⸀ὡς καὶ ἡμεῖς;
verses.mistral: Can the water prevent someone from being baptized, who received the Holy Spirit as we did?
verses.kjv: Can any man forbid water, that these should not be baptized, which have received the Holy Ghost as well as we?

verses.greek: προσέταξεν ⸀δὲ αὐτοὺς ⸂ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ βαπτισθῆναι⸃. τότε ἠρώτησαν αὐτὸν ἐπιμεῖναι ἡμέρας τινάς.
verses.mistral: Directed them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked him to stay for some days.
verses.kjv: And he commanded them to be baptized in the name of the Lord. Then prayed they him to tarry certain days.

verses.greek: Ἤκουσαν δὲ οἱ ἀπόστολοι καὶ οἱ ἀδελφοὶ οἱ ὄντες κατὰ τὴν Ἰουδαίαν ὅτι καὶ τὰ ἔθνη ἐδέξαντο τὸν λόγον τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: Listened then the apostles and the brethren who were dwelling in Judea, that even the Gentiles had received the word of God.
verses.kjv: And the apostles and brethren that were in Judaea heard that the Gentiles had also received the word of God.

verses.greek: ⸂ὅτε δὲ⸃ ἀνέβη Πέτρος εἰς ⸀Ἰερουσαλήμ, διεκρίνοντο πρὸς αὐτὸν οἱ ἐκ περιτομῆς
verses.mistral: When Peter came up to Jerusalem, those of the circumcision contended with him
verses.kjv: And when Peter was come up to Jerusalem, they that were of the circumcision contended with him,

verses.greek: λέγοντες ὅτι ⸂Εἰσῆλθες πρὸς ἄνδρας ἀκροβυστίαν ἔχοντας καὶ συνέφαγες⸃ αὐτοῖς.
verses.mistral: They said, 'You went in to uncircumcised men and ate with them.'
verses.kjv: Saying, Thou wentest in to men uncircumcised, and didst eat with them.

verses.greek: ἀρξάμενος ⸀δὲ Πέτρος ἐξετίθετο αὐτοῖς καθεξῆς λέγων·
verses.mistral: Began Peter, and was being explained to them in order saying:
verses.kjv: But Peter rehearsed the matter from the beginning, and expounded it by order unto them, saying,

verses.greek: Ἐγὼ ἤμην ἐν πόλει Ἰόππῃ προσευχόμενος καὶ εἶδον ἐν ἐκστάσει ὅραμα, καταβαῖνον σκεῦός τι ὡς ὀθόνην μεγάλην τέσσαρσιν ἀρχαῖς καθιεμένην ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ, καὶ ἦλθεν ἄχρι ἐμοῦ·
verses.mistral: I was in the city of Joppa praying, and in a trance I saw a vision. A certain object descended like a great sheet let down from heaven by four corners, and it came to me.
verses.kjv: I was in the city of Joppa praying: and in a trance I saw a vision, A certain vessel descend, as it had been a great sheet, let down from heaven by four corners; and it came even to me:

verses.greek: εἰς ἣν ἀτενίσας κατενόουν καὶ εἶδον τὰ τετράποδα τῆς γῆς καὶ τὰ θηρία καὶ τὰ ἑρπετὰ καὶ τὰ πετεινὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ·
verses.mistral: Creatures of the earth, beasts, creeping things, and flying creatures of heaven.
verses.kjv: Upon the which when I had fastened mine eyes, I considered, and saw fourfooted beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and fowls of the air.

verses.greek: ἤκουσα δὲ ⸀καὶ φωνῆς λεγούσης μοι· Ἀναστάς, Πέτρε, θῦσον καὶ φάγε.
verses.mistral: Rise, Peter, kill and eat.
verses.kjv: And I heard a voice saying unto me, Arise, Peter; slay and eat.

verses.greek: εἶπον δέ· Μηδαμῶς, κύριε, ⸀ὅτι κοινὸν ἢ ἀκάθαρτον οὐδέποτε εἰσῆλθεν εἰς τὸ στόμα μου.
verses.mistral: Never, Lord, have I ever eaten anything that is common or unclean.
verses.kjv: But I said, Not so, Lord: for nothing common or unclean hath at any time entered into my mouth.

verses.greek: ἀπεκρίθη δὲ ⸂φωνὴ ἐκ δευτέρου⸃ ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ· Ἃ ὁ θεὸς ἐκαθάρισεν σὺ μὴ κοίνου.
verses.mistral: Clean animal thou hast God made.
verses.kjv: But the voice answered me again from heaven, What God hath cleansed, that call not thou common.

verses.greek: τοῦτο δὲ ἐγένετο ἐπὶ τρίς, καὶ ⸂ἀνεσπάσθη πάλιν⸃ ἅπαντα εἰς τὸν οὐρανόν.
verses.mistral: This occurred three times, and all were taken up into heaven.
verses.kjv: And this was done three times: and all were drawn up again into heaven.

verses.greek: καὶ ἰδοὺ ἐξαυτῆς τρεῖς ἄνδρες ἐπέστησαν ἐπὶ τὴν οἰκίαν ἐν ᾗ ⸀ἦμεν, ἀπεσταλμένοι ἀπὸ Καισαρείας πρός με.
verses.mistral: Immediately, three men stood up before our house, sent from Caesarea to me.
verses.kjv: And, behold, immediately there were three men already come unto the house where I was, sent from Caesarea unto me.

verses.greek: εἶπεν δὲ ⸂τὸ πνεῦμά μοι⸃ συνελθεῖν αὐτοῖς μηδὲν ⸀διακρίναντα. ἦλθον δὲ σὺν ἐμοὶ καὶ οἱ ἓξ ἀδελφοὶ οὗτοι, καὶ εἰσήλθομεν εἰς τὸν οἶκον τοῦ ἀνδρός.
verses.mistral: The Spirit said to me, "Go with them without distinguishing anything." So these seven went with me, and we entered the man's house.
verses.kjv: And the Spirit bade me go with them, nothing doubting. Moreover these six brethren accompanied me, and we entered into the man's house:

verses.greek: ἀπήγγειλεν ⸀δὲ ἡμῖν πῶς εἶδεν τὸν ἄγγελον ἐν τῷ οἴκῳ αὐτοῦ σταθέντα καὶ ⸀εἰπόντα· Ἀπόστειλον εἰς ⸀Ἰόππην καὶ μετάπεμψαι Σίμωνα τὸν ἐπικαλούμενον Πέτρον,
verses.mistral: Announced to us how he saw the angel standing in his house and saying, "Send to Joppa and summon Simon called Peter."
verses.kjv: And he shewed us how he had seen an angel in his house, which stood and said unto him, Send men to Joppa, and call for Simon, whose surname is Peter;

verses.greek: ὃς λαλήσει ῥήματα πρὸς σὲ ἐν οἷς σωθήσῃ σὺ καὶ πᾶς ὁ οἶκός σου.
verses.mistral: He who speaks to you in these words will save you and all your household.
verses.kjv: Who shall tell thee words, whereby thou and all thy house shall be saved.

verses.greek: ἐν δὲ τῷ ἄρξασθαί με λαλεῖν ἐπέπεσεν τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον ἐπ’ αὐτοὺς ὥσπερ καὶ ἐφ’ ἡμᾶς ἐν ἀρχῇ.
verses.mistral: The Holy Spirit fell upon them, just as it did on us at the beginning.
verses.kjv: And as I began to speak, the Holy Ghost fell on them, as on us at the beginning.

verses.greek: ἐμνήσθην δὲ τοῦ ῥήματος ⸀τοῦ κυρίου ὡς ἔλεγεν· Ἰωάννης μὲν ἐβάπτισεν ὕδατι, ὑμεῖς δὲ βαπτισθήσεσθε ἐν πνεύματι ἁγίῳ.
verses.mistral: Remember the word of the Lord, as he said: John baptized with water, but you will be baptized in the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: Then remembered I the word of the Lord, how that he said, John indeed baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost.

verses.greek: εἰ οὖν τὴν ἴσην δωρεὰν ἔδωκεν αὐτοῖς ὁ θεὸς ὡς καὶ ἡμῖν πιστεύσασιν ἐπὶ τὸν κύριον Ἰησοῦν Χριστόν, ⸀ἐγὼ τίς ἤμην δυνατὸς κωλῦσαι τὸν θεόν;
verses.mistral: If God gave them the same gift as He did to us who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, whom am I that I should be able to hinder God?
verses.kjv: Forasmuch then as God gave them the like gift as he did unto us, who believed on the Lord Jesus Christ; what was I, that I could withstand God?

verses.greek: ἀκούσαντες δὲ ταῦτα ἡσύχασαν καὶ ⸀ἐδόξασαν τὸν θεὸν λέγοντες· ⸀Ἄρα καὶ τοῖς ἔθνεσιν ὁ θεὸς τὴν μετάνοιαν ⸂εἰς ζωὴν ἔδωκεν⸃.
verses.mistral: Hearing this, they fell silent and glorified God, saying, "Indeed, even to the Gentiles has God granted repentance unto life.
verses.kjv: When they heard these things, they held their peace, and glorified God, saying, Then hath God also to the Gentiles granted repentance unto life.

verses.greek: Οἱ μὲν οὖν διασπαρέντες ἀπὸ τῆς θλίψεως τῆς γενομένης ἐπὶ Στεφάνῳ διῆλθον ἕως Φοινίκης καὶ Κύπρου καὶ Ἀντιοχείας μηδενὶ λαλοῦντες τὸν λόγον εἰ μὴ μόνον Ἰουδαίοις.
verses.mistral: Dispersed from the persecution that occurred with Stephen, they went as far as Phoenicia and Cyprus and Antioch, speaking to no one except Jews.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now they which were scattered abroad upon the persecution that arose about Stephen travelled as far as Phenice, and Cyprus, and Antioch, preaching the word to none but unto the Jews only.

verses.greek: ἦσαν δέ τινες ἐξ αὐτῶν ἄνδρες Κύπριοι καὶ Κυρηναῖοι, οἵτινες ⸀ἐλθόντες εἰς Ἀντιόχειαν ἐλάλουν ⸀καὶ πρὸς τοὺς ⸀Ἑλληνιστάς, εὐαγγελιζόμενοι τὸν κύριον Ἰησοῦν.
verses.mistral: Some Cyprus and Cypriot men among them went to Antioch and began speaking to the Greeks, proclaiming the Lord Jesus.
verses.kjv: And some of them were men of Cyprus and Cyrene, which, when they were come to Antioch, spake unto the Grecians, preaching the Lord Jesus.

verses.greek: καὶ ἦν χεὶρ κυρίου μετ’ αὐτῶν, πολύς τε ἀριθμὸς ⸀ὁ πιστεύσας ἐπέστρεψεν ἐπὶ τὸν κύριον.
verses.mistral: And the hand of the Lord was with them, a great number who believed turned to the Lord.
verses.kjv: And the hand of the Lord was with them: and a great number believed, and turned unto the Lord.

verses.greek: ἠκούσθη δὲ ὁ λόγος εἰς τὰ ὦτα τῆς ἐκκλησίας τῆς ⸀οὔσης ἐν ⸀Ἰερουσαλὴμ περὶ αὐτῶν, καὶ ἐξαπέστειλαν ⸀Βαρναβᾶν ἕως Ἀντιοχείας·
verses.mistral: The word was heard in the church that was in Jerusalem about them, and they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then tidings of these things came unto the ears of the church which was in Jerusalem: and they sent forth Barnabas, that he should go as far as Antioch.

verses.greek: ὃς παραγενόμενος καὶ ἰδὼν τὴν χάριν ⸀τὴν τοῦ θεοῦ, ἐχάρη καὶ παρεκάλει πάντας τῇ προθέσει τῆς καρδίας ⸀προσμένειν τῷ κυρίῳ,
verses.mistral: Arriving and seeing the grace of God, he rejoiced and encouraged all to remain faithful to the Lord
verses.kjv: Who, when he came, and had seen the grace of God, was glad, and exhorted them all, that with purpose of heart they would cleave unto the Lord.

verses.greek: ὅτι ἦν ἀνὴρ ἀγαθὸς καὶ πλήρης πνεύματος ἁγίου καὶ πίστεως. καὶ προσετέθη ὄχλος ἱκανὸς τῷ κυρίῳ.
verses.mistral: Good man, full of the Holy Spirit and faith, he was also added to the Lord, and a sufficient number of the people were saved.
verses.kjv: For he was a good man, and full of the Holy Ghost and of faith: and much people was added unto the Lord.

verses.greek: ἐξῆλθεν δὲ εἰς ⸀Ταρσὸν ἀναζητῆσαι Σαῦλον,
verses.mistral: Departed then to Tarsus, seeking Saul
verses.kjv: Then departed Barnabas to Tarsus, for to seek Saul:

verses.greek: καὶ εὑρὼν ⸀ἤγαγεν εἰς Ἀντιόχειαν. ἐγένετο δὲ ⸂αὐτοῖς καὶ⸃ ἐνιαυτὸν ὅλον συναχθῆναι ⸀ἐν τῇ ἐκκλησίᾳ καὶ διδάξαι ὄχλον ἱκανόν, χρηματίσαι τε ⸀πρώτως ἐν Ἀντιοχείᾳ τοὺς μαθητὰς Χριστιανούς.
verses.mistral: Found and brought Saul to Antioch. It happened that for a whole year they assembled together in the church and taught the crowd sufficiently, first in Antioch they supported the disciples who were called Christians.
verses.kjv: And when he had found him, he brought him unto Antioch. And it came to pass, that a whole year they assembled themselves with the church, and taught much people. And the disciples were called Christians first in Antioch.

verses.greek: Ἐν ταύταις δὲ ταῖς ἡμέραις κατῆλθον ἀπὸ Ἱεροσολύμων προφῆται εἰς Ἀντιόχειαν·
verses.mistral: Prophets came from Jerusalem to Antioch in these days.
verses.kjv: ¶ And in these days came prophets from Jerusalem unto Antioch.

verses.greek: ἀναστὰς δὲ εἷς ἐξ αὐτῶν ὀνόματι Ἅγαβος ἐσήμανεν διὰ τοῦ πνεύματος λιμὸν ⸀μεγάλην μέλλειν ἔσεσθαι ἐφ’ ὅλην τὴν οἰκουμένην· ⸀ἥτις ἐγένετο ἐπὶ ⸀Κλαυδίου.
verses.mistral: One of them named Agabus stood up and predicted by the Spirit that there would be a great famine all over the world beginning in the days of Claudius.
verses.kjv: And there stood up one of them named Agabus, and signified by the Spirit that there should be great dearth throughout all the world: which came to pass in the days of Claudius Caesar.

verses.greek: τῶν δὲ μαθητῶν καθὼς εὐπορεῖτό τις ὥρισαν ἕκαστος αὐτῶν εἰς διακονίαν πέμψαι τοῖς κατοικοῦσιν ἐν τῇ Ἰουδαίᾳ ἀδελφοῖς·
verses.mistral: The disciples, as it was determined by each one of them, decided to send aid to the Jews living in Judea.
verses.kjv: Then the disciples, every man according to his ability, determined to send relief unto the brethren which dwelt in Judaea:

verses.greek: ὃ καὶ ἐποίησαν ἀποστείλαντες πρὸς τοὺς πρεσβυτέρους διὰ χειρὸς Βαρναβᾶ καὶ Σαύλου.
verses.mistral: They sent, through the hands of Barnabas and Saul.
verses.kjv: Which also they did, and sent it to the elders by the hands of Barnabas and Saul.

verses.greek: Κατ’ ἐκεῖνον δὲ τὸν καιρὸν ἐπέβαλεν Ἡρῴδης ὁ βασιλεὺς τὰς χεῖρας κακῶσαί τινας τῶν ἀπὸ τῆς ἐκκλησίας.
verses.mistral: Herod the king began to mistreat some of those from the church.
verses.kjv: Now about that time Herod the king stretched forth his hands to vex certain of the church.

verses.greek: ἀνεῖλεν δὲ Ἰάκωβον τὸν ἀδελφὸν Ἰωάννου μαχαίρῃ.
verses.mistral: Executed Jacob, the brother of John, with a sword.
verses.kjv: And he killed James the brother of John with the sword.
